feed.title: Is Fastenal (FAST) Pricing In Too Much Growth After Its Strong Multi‑Year Run?
feed.summary: Fastenal's stock performance has seen significant gains over the past five years, but current valuation checks suggest it may be overvalued. A Discounted Cash Flow analysis indicates the stock is about 112% above its estimated intrinsic value, and its P/E ratio of 42.40x is well above the industry average and Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio of 32.82x. The article explores bull and bear case scenarios, offering different fair value perspectives based on varying assumptions about revenue growth and operational factors.

feed.title: Campbell’s Soup factory in Paris to lay off 205 workers
feed.summary: The Campbell's Soup factory in Paris, Texas, is planning to lay off 205 of its 568 employees as it transitions from soup production to manufacturing Prego Italian sauces and Pace salsa. Soup production will cease on May 1st, and employees who remain until their layoff date will be eligible for separation benefits. The factory has been operating since 1964.

feed.title: Do New AI Chip Export Risks Undermine Applied Materials' (AMAT) S&P 100 Moment?
feed.summary: Applied Materials is facing increased investor caution due to geopolitical tensions, AI-chip export controls, and an Erste Group hold rating, despite its recent inclusion in the S&P 100. The company's investment narrative depends on AI-driven demand outweighing these regulatory pressures, with forecasts projecting significant revenue and earnings growth by 2028. While index inclusion offers passive fund demand, fundamental risks like export licensing and customer concentration remain.

feed.title: Honeywell Spin Offs Aerospace Unit And Reshapes Valuation Outlook
feed.summary: Honeywell International is spinning off its Aerospace division into a separate publicly traded company, a move that will create two focused entities with distinct priorities. The company has filed necessary SEC documents and arranged financing for the separation. This transaction will impact Honeywell's earnings mix, risk profile, and future valuation, urging investors to closely monitor details like debt levels and new dividend policies for both entities.

feed.title: New hybrid system brings electric efficiency to regional aircraft
feed.summary: RTX is developing a new combined thermal/electric propulsion system for regional turboprop aircraft, aiming to significantly boost efficiency. This hybrid system, which can be retrofitted into existing aircraft, integrates a Pratt & Whitney turboprop engine with a Collins Aerospace electric motor, allowing both to simultaneously drive the propeller shaft. The goal is to reduce fuel consumption by 30% and maintenance costs by 20%, while also enabling the use of 100% Sustainable Aviation Fuel.

feed.title: Ranpak Holdings Earnings Call Highlights Automation Pivot
feed.summary: Ranpak Holdings (PACK) held its Q4 earnings call, revealing solid operational progress mixed with margin and regional demand pressures. The company showcased strong performance in North America and accelerated growth in its automation segment, which broke even on an Adjusted EBITDA basis in Q4. Ranpak also announced ambitious automation targets for 2026, aiming for 30%-50% revenue growth, and highlighted significant strategic partnerships and investments in AI and robotics to drive future expansion despite ongoing profitability pressures and elevated leverage.

feed.title: Conagra Brands expanding Fayetteville production facility, AEDC says
feed.summary: Conagra Brands is expanding its Fayetteville, Arkansas manufacturing facility through a $220 million multi-year investment. This expansion aims to increase chicken production capacity and is expected to create 100 new jobs over the next five years, boosting the region's economy and manufacturing workforce. Construction is planned to begin in late 2026, reinforcing Conagra's commitment to the local community and its frozen foods business growth.

feed.title: Sheinbaum Urges Coca-Cola to Increase Use of Mexican Cane Sugar
feed.summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum has urged The Coca-Cola Company to use more Mexican cane sugar in their soft drinks instead of imported fructose, aiming to support the domestic sugar industry. This proposal aligns with efforts to strengthen national agricultural supply chains and addresses concerns about low sugar prices and rising sweetener imports. Sheinbaum also discussed water management and environmental impact with Coca-Cola executives, emphasizing that new industrial plants must adhere to strict environmental regulations.

feed.title: Consumers Select Owens Corning as America’s Most Trusted® Insulation Brand for 2026
feed.summary: Owens Corning has been named America's Most Trusted® Insulation Brand for 2026 by Lifestory Research. The company achieved the highest trust rating among leading insulation brands with a Net Trust Quotient Score of 111.7, based on feedback from over 4,400 consumers. This recognition highlights consumer confidence in Owens Corning's product performance, reliability, and long-term value.

feed.title: Ford recalls 1.74 million of its cars over rearview camera display issues
feed.summary: Ford has recalled nearly 1.74 million vehicles in the U.S. due to software issues affecting rearview camera displays. The recalls include 2021-2026 Ford Broncos and 2021-2024 Ford Edges facing infotainment system overheating, and 2020-2022 Ford Escapes, Lincoln Corsairs, 2020-2024 Lincoln Aviators, and Explorers which may display inverted rearview images. While no injuries or accidents have been reported, the NHTSA warns that these issues could increase crash risks, and Ford is working on software updates and remedies for the affected models.

feed.title: Ford recalls 1.74 million of its cars over rearview camera display issues
feed.summary: Ford is recalling nearly 1.74 million vehicles in the U.S. due to two separate rearview camera display issues that could increase crash risks. One recall affects certain 2021-2026 Ford Broncos and 2021-2024 Ford Edges where the infotainment system may overheat, preventing the rearview image from displaying. The second recall impacts 2020-2022 Ford Escapes and Lincoln Corsairs, and 2020-2024 Lincoln Aviators and Explorers, where the rearview image may appear flipped or inverted.

These moves position Parker-Hannifin for long-term growth by aligning with key industry trends and potentially supporting resilient earnings, though acquisition risks and debt levels warrant investor attention.

feed.title: Cleveland Steel Showdown: Ohio Targets Cliffs And Duck Creek Over Radioactive Mess
feed.summary: Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost has filed a lawsuit against Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. and Duck Creek Energy, accusing them of mishandling radioactive waste at Cleveland-Cliffs' Cleveland Works steel mill. The complaint alleges improper disposal of brine, oil, and radioactive materials, leading to contamination and potential environmental violations. This state action escalates a prior legal dispute where Duck Creek sued Cleveland-Cliffs, and seeks to hold both companies accountable for cleanup and compliance.

feed.title: Germany Advancing 5 Billion Euro Rocket Launcher Deal With Israel's Elbit
feed.summary: The German government is moving forward with a deal to acquire rocket-launcher systems from Israel's Elbit Systems, valued at over five billion euros. This acquisition, if finalized, would involve hundreds of rocket launchers, significantly increasing Elbit's order backlog by approximately 20 percent and strengthening its presence in the European defense market. The specific time of publication is 05:54 PM IST on March 07, 2026.

feed.title: ‘Rags soiled with hazardous waste’ found in bags. N.J. recycler on the hook for six-figure fine.
feed.summary: A Linden, New Jersey recycling center, Safety-Kleen Systems, a subsidiary of Clean Harbors, has been fined $175,000 by the EPA following an inspection in May 2024. Inspectors found numerous violations, including improper maintenance, leaking equipment, faulty storage, and hazardous waste in incompatible containers, posing threats to human health and the environment. The company has since worked with the EPA to address concerns and implement corrective measures.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson will spend $1 billion on a cell therapy plant in Montgomery County
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson is investing $1 billion in building a new manufacturing plant for cell therapy in Montgomery County. This significant investment highlights the company's commitment to advancing cell therapy production and expanding its footprint in the biotechnology sector. The new facility is expected to bolster J&J's capacity to develop and produce innovative cell therapy treatments.

feed.title: Agricultural Industry Champion Roger Hoy Retires, Eric Smith Named Director of Nebraska Tractor Test Lab
feed.summary: Roger Hoy has retired as Director of the Nebraska Tractor Test Lab (NTTL) after nearly two decades, passing the leadership to Eric Smith, a fellow John Deere veteran. Hoy's tenure was marked by a deep understanding of industry and academia, bridging manufacturers, regulators, and farmers, while mentoring students and forecasting automation as agriculture's most disruptive technology. Smith, with extensive experience in agricultural engineering and standards, is set to continue the lab's mission of rigorous testing, industry partnership, and educational outreach for future engineers, with a focus on electrification and automation.

feed.title: NIO's Firefly officially enters Thailand as its 2nd right-hand drive market
feed.summary: NIO's Firefly sub-brand has officially launched in Thailand, marking its second right-hand drive market after Singapore, as part of NIO's global expansion into tariff-free territories. The launch was in partnership with Thonburi BlueSky Co., Ltd., with specific Thai pricing and variants expected to be announced later in March. The Firefly is a five-door subcompact hatchback that has accumulated over 40,000 global deliveries since its Chinese launch in April 2025 and is set to expand to over 40 markets worldwide.

feed.title: Wabtec Settlement With Progress Rail Clears Path For Rail Growth
feed.summary: Wabtec (NYSE:WAB) and Progress Rail have settled their legal dispute, removing uncertainty for Wabtec's rail business and allowing both companies to focus on core rail industry themes. This resolution is seen as beneficial for investors, enabling Wabtec to prioritize contract execution, aftermarket support, and digital solutions, aligning with the narrative of rail modernization and growth. However, investors should also consider the risks of lagging organic revenue growth and reliance on acquisitions amidst this positive development.

feed.title: Does Onshape’s New Cloud MBD Capability Deepen PTC’s (PTC) Role in End‑to‑End Product Lifecycles?
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has launched a cloud-native Model-Based Definition (MBD) capability within its Onshape CAD and PDM platform, allowing engineering teams to embed manufacturing information directly into 3D models. This move aims to centralize product and manufacturing data, enhancing PTC's role in customers' end-to-end product lifecycles and AI-driven process improvements. While it strengthens PTC's intelligent product lifecycle story and growth catalysts, the article notes that investors should also consider ongoing risks from competition and market uncertainty.

feed.title: Does Onshape’s New Cloud MBD Capability Deepen PTC’s (PTC) Role in End‑to‑End Product Lifecycles?
feed.summary: PTC Inc. has introduced a new cloud-native Model-Based Definition (MBD) capability within its Onshape CAD and PDM platform. feed.title: Cummins' Fastest-Growing Business Isn't Trucks. It's Data Center Power.

Despite a cyclical slowdown in its traditional engine and components businesses, Cummins' overall financial performance is strong, and its stock is considered a reasonable play on the AI sector.

feed.title: Brazil Steel Industry: Gerdau Warns of Import Pressure, Seeks Trade Measures - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Gerdau, a leading Brazilian steelmaker, has voiced concerns over the escalating volume of steel imports, projecting they will exceed six million tons in 2025, constituting nearly 30% of domestic sales. The company warns that without protective trade measures, many firms could face financial distress and operational struggles. Gerdau is optimistic about improved government attention to this issue and anticipates a market expansion driven by growth in the automotive sector and sustained construction activity, aligning with their recent investment in restarting a billet production facility.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Introduces LX4580 Mixed-Signal IC for Aviation and Defense Actuation Systems
feed.summary: Microchip Technology has launched the LX4580, a highly integrated 24-channel mixed-signal IC designed to streamline actuation control systems in aviation and defense. This single chip consolidates multiple functions, reducing system size, weight, and complexity for applications like More Electric Aircraft and guided defense systems. The device also offers a redundant architecture for safety-critical applications and comes with an evaluation board and documentation to accelerate design cycles.

feed.title: Johnson Controls shareholders approve board elections and key proposals at AGM
feed.summary: Johnson Controls International plc shareholders approved several key proposals at their annual general meeting, including the election of directors, ratification of auditors, authorization for share repurchases, and an advisory vote on executive compensation. The board size was reduced from 12 to 11 members following a director's decision not to seek re-election. Shareholders also authorized the board to allot shares up to an aggregate nominal value of $1,286,103 and waived statutory pre-emption rights for new share issuances for cash.

feed.title: Methode Electronics, Inc. (NYSE:MEI) Q3 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Methode Electronics, Inc. (NYSE:MEI) reported sales of $234 million and adjusted EBITDA of $7.3 million for Q3 fiscal 2026, with industrial segments showing growth despite overall revenue pressure. feed.title: Methanex Corporation (NASDAQ:MEOH) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Methanex Corporation discussed its Q4 2025 financial results, reporting an adjusted EBITDA of $180 million and an adjusted net loss of $11 million, with an average realized price of $331 per tonne. feed.title: Lockheed Martin Goes Full Throttle On Weapons Production— Commits To 4X Output After Trump Meeting
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) has announced a commitment to quadruple its munitions production following a meeting with former President Donald Trump and other defense industry leaders. This expansion, initiated several months prior with Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Deputy Secretary Stephen Feinberg, aims to enhance the U.S. military's capabilities. The move comes amidst active conflicts involving the U.S. and Israel, and Lockheed Martin is also aligning with Trump's directives regarding the use of AI tools.

feed.title: Northrop Grumman Rare Earth Shift Tests Compliance Costs And Valuation

feed.title: CarParts.com 2025 Financials: Quarterly and Annual Revenue & Loss - News and Statistics
feed.summary: CarParts.com reported a Q4 2025 loss of $11.6 million on $120.4 million in revenue, resulting in a full-year loss of $50.4 million with annual revenue of $547.5 million. The report also details comprehensive market analysis for the bumper industry in the United States, covering demand, supply, trade flows, pricing, and forecasts up to 2035. This industry analysis aims to support strategic planning and risk management for manufacturers, distributors, and investors in the automotive sector.

feed.title: Valmont Industries appoints KPMG as new auditor, dismisses Deloitte By Investing.com
feed.summary: Valmont Industries (NYSE:VMI) has appointed KPMG LLP as its new independent registered public accounting firm, effective for the fiscal year ending December 26, 2026, and interim periods. This decision, approved by the company's Audit Committee, follows the dismissal of Deloitte & Touche LLP, with no reported disagreements or reportable events with either firm. In other news, Valmont recently reported its Q4 2025 earnings, increased its quarterly dividend by 13%, and appointed Paul T. Maass to its board of directors.

feed.title: How Is W.W. Grainger's Stock Performance Compared to Other Industrial Stocks?
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger, Inc. (GWW) is a large-cap industrial distributor with a market cap of $54.3 billion, actively integrating AI and machine learning into its operations. While GWW has outperformed the iShares U.S. Industrials ETF (IYJ) in the short term (up 14.1% in three months and 10.3% YTD), it has underperformed IYJ and its rival Fastenal Company (FAST) over the past 52 weeks. Analysts currently maintain a "Hold" rating on GWW, with a mean price target suggesting a slight premium to its current price.

feed.title: Caterpillar Stock Drop Looks Sharp, But How Deep Can It Go?
feed.summary: Caterpillar (CAT) stock has recently dropped 8.3% over five trading days due to valuation concerns and rising tariff costs. Despite this, the company exhibits moderate operational performance and high valuation, making the stock currently unattractive based on Trefis metrics. The article further analyzes CAT's historical performance during economic downturns, showing that it has often fared worse than the S&P 500 but generally recovered, albeit sometimes slower than the broader market.

feed.title: Palladyne AI Forecasts Fourfold Revenue Growth In 2026
feed.summary: Palladyne AI (NASDAQ: PDYN) shares surged after the company projected a 357% to 415% year-over-year revenue growth for 2026, targeting $24 million to $27 million. This optimistic outlook is backed by a 118% jump in Q4 sales to $1.66 million and a rising backlog, despite missing Q4 adjusted loss consensus. The company, which specializes in AI and autonomous systems for defense, also highlighted recent acquisitions, product launches like Palladyne IQ 2.0, and a new contract supporting a U.S. missile system.

feed.title: Stabilis Solutions Faces High Execution, Financing and Demand Risks on Proposed Galveston LNG Project
feed.summary: Stabilis Solutions, Inc. (SLNG) faces significant execution, financing, and demand risks for its proposed Galveston LNG liquefaction facility. The project requires substantial upfront capital, with no cash inflows until completion, and is vulnerable to construction delays, cost overruns, and permitting issues. Additionally, the viability of the facility depends on LNG demand materializing and securing attractive contracts, as failure in these areas could negatively impact the company's financial condition.

feed.title: Hoffman, Century Aluminum SVP, IT and CAO, sells $563,300 in stock
feed.summary: Robert F. Hoffman, SVP, IT and CAO at Century Aluminum Co (CENX), recently sold 10,000 shares of common stock for $563,300, with shares trading near their 52-week high after a 188% return over the past year. This insider transaction comes amidst positive news for Century Aluminum, including a strategic partnership for a new production plant in Oklahoma and increased analyst price targets, despite some impact from potential tariff changes. Hoffman retains a substantial holding in the company, including vested and unvested shares.

feed.title: Assessing RBC Bearings (RBC) Valuation After Strong Multi Year Shareholder Returns
feed.summary: RBC Bearings (RBC) has seen strong shareholder returns over the past three years, with a 149.02% total return. Despite a recent short-term pullback, the company's valuation narrative suggests it is 7% undervalued at $594.50, driven by anticipated revenue growth and improved margins from capacity expansions in aerospace and defense. However, its high P/E ratio of 65.1x raises questions about whether the market has already factored in future growth and if the valuation is sustainable.

feed.title: Is Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) Pricing Look Attractive After Recent Share Price Pullback
feed.summary: Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) has experienced recent share price declines but appears undervalued based on two key valuation approaches. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests a 57.9% discount to its intrinsic value, while its current P/E ratio of 16.7x is below both industry and peer averages, further indicating undervaluation. The article encourages investors to use valuation narratives on their platform to form their own assessment of the company's future prospects.

feed.title: Electrovaya ranked 3rd in Canada's Enterprise-Industry Leaders winners in Deloitte's Technology Fast 50TM program
feed.summary: Electrovaya has been recognized for the third consecutive year in Deloitte's Technology Fast 50TM program, ranking 3rd in the Enterprise-Industry Leaders category. The company achieved a 383% growth over three years, thanks to the increasing adoption of its Infinity Battery technology. This award highlights Electrovaya's strong revenue growth and its innovative contributions to the lithium-ion battery sector, especially for mission-critical applications.

feed.title: Orion SA Schedules Hybrid 2026 Annual Shareholders Meeting
feed.summary: Orion S.A. has scheduled its 2026 annual general meeting of shareholders for June 25, 2026, to be held in a hybrid format, allowing both in-person attendance in Luxembourg and virtual participation via live webcast. The record date for shareholders is April 23, 2026. Despite a "Hold" analyst rating and concerns from TipRanks' AI Analyst "Spark" regarding profitability and leverage, the hybrid format aims to enhance accessibility and engagement for its global investor base.

feed.title: Orion S.A. Announces its 2026 Annual General Meeting Date and Record Date
feed.summary: Orion S.A. announced that its 2026 annual general meeting of shareholders will be held on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at 2 p.m. Central European Time in Luxembourg. The company also set April 23, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. Central European Time as the record date for shareholder admission to the meeting. The meeting will offer both in-person attendance and a live online webcast option.

feed.title: SES AI Earnings Call: Fast Growth, Cautious Outlook
feed.summary: SES AI Corporation reported impressive nearly tenfold revenue growth and improved margins in 2025, largely driven by high-margin service revenue that is not expected to recur. While the company demonstrated strong cost discipline and maintains a robust liquidity position, challenges remain in achieving sustained hardware-led growth, with EV battery commercialization currently paused due to market conditions. The outlook for 2026 projects continued top-line growth but acknowledges persistent losses and the need to scale manufacturing beyond current pilot capacities.

feed.title: Advanced Drainage Systems raises FY26 revenue guidance to $2.945B amid strong Allied and Infiltrator product growth
feed.summary: Advanced Drainage Systems (ADS) has increased its fiscal year 2026 revenue guidance to $2.945 billion. This upward revision is attributed to robust growth in its Allied Products and Infiltrator brands. The company anticipates continued strong performance from these key product lines.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Posts Robust Profits Amid Investor Doubts, Placing 223rd Among 600 Million Shares Traded
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams (SHW) closed down 1.66% at $335.32 per share, despite a 5.6% increase in annual revenue and beating EPS expectations. The company's stock is trading below its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, reflecting investor doubts despite strong financial results like a 65.26% return on equity. Mixed institutional and insider activity, along with concerns over debt levels and market competition, contribute to its volatile performance, even as analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus.

feed.title: Orion S.A. shareholders get June 25 AGM with virtual access
feed.summary: Orion S.A. (NYSE: OEC) announced its 2026 annual general meeting (AGM) of shareholders will be held on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at 2 p.m. Central European Time in Luxembourg. The record date for shareholder admission is April 23, 2026. The meeting will offer both in-person attendance and a live online webcast for virtual participation.

feed.title: Howmet Gains From Strength in Commercial Aerospace: Will the Momentum Last?
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (HWM) is experiencing significant growth, primarily driven by its strong performance in the commercial aerospace market, which accounted for 53% of its Q4 2025 revenues. The increase in air travel and demand for more fuel-efficient aircraft are key factors contributing to this growth, with the defense sector also showing positive momentum. Despite trading at a higher forward price-to-earnings ratio than its industry average, analyst estimates for Howmet's earnings have been rising, and the company currently holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

feed.title: Analog Devices Inc.: Is This Quiet Chip Giant Your Next Big Tech Play?
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) is presented as a high-quality semiconductor company that is crucial for AI, EVs, industrial automation, and defense sectors. Despite not being a widely recognized consumer brand, its chips are integral to these technologies, offering a strong investment opportunity for those looking beyond flashy AI names. Analysts view ADI as a core analog holding due to its robust engineering, exposure to structural growth, healthy margins, and resilience compared to more volatile chipmakers.

feed.title: Myers Industries Sees Growth in Military Products Amid Global Geopolitical Tensions
feed.summary: Myers Industries is experiencing significant growth in its military product segment, particularly ammunition packaging, driven by increased global geopolitical tensions and defense spending. The company's dual supply chain in the US and Canada allows it to service both US and NATO military customers. Despite a flat year-over-year in net sales for Q4 2025 due to exiting low-margin products, Myers Industries remains optimistic, with strategic investments and a "Focus Transformation Program" aiming for long-term growth.

feed.title: ArcelorMittal announces the publication of its Annual Report 2025 on Form 20 F and the publication of its 2025 annual report
feed.summary: ArcelorMittal has filed its Annual Report 2025 on Form 20-F with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and published its annual report for the year ended December 31, 2025. Key highlights for fiscal year 2025 include improved safety KPIs, disciplined capital allocation with investments in strategic capex and returns to shareholders, enhanced iron ore vertical integration, active enablement of energy transition through targeted investments, and continued commitment to R&D. The company also proposed a higher dividend for FY 2026 and detailed a share repurchase agreement with a Significant Shareholder.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Mueller Industries (MLI) 40% Quarterly Dividend Increase
feed.summary: Mueller Industries (MLI) has raised its quarterly cash dividend by 40% to US$0.35 per share, signaling a focus on returning value to shareholders. While this dividend hike reinforces the company's appeal for income-oriented investors, the article notes that underlying risks like slower projected earnings growth and recent insider selling remain considerations. The stock is currently trading below some fair value estimates, and the increased payout is expected to attract income-focused buyers.

feed.title: Ford Recalls 1.74 Million SUVs Due to Rear Camera Issues
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has issued two separate recalls affecting 1.74 million late-model Ford and Lincoln SUVs due to rear camera malfunctions. One recall impacts 849,310 Ford Broncos (2021-2016) and Ford Edge (2021-2024) vehicles, where the rearview camera may temporarily shut down, leading to a blank screen. The second recall covers 889,950 Ford Escape (2020-2022), Lincoln Corsair (2020-2022), and Lincoln Aviator (2020-2024) models, facing issues with inverted or flipped camera images on the touchscreen. Both issues are expected to be resolved with software updates, though the fixes are not yet available.

feed.title: Jabil Inc. Stock Is Quietly Pivoting: What You Need to Know Now
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. is making significant strategic moves, shifting its focus from lower-margin businesses to high-growth areas like EVs, healthcare, and AI hardware. The company acts as a crucial behind-the-scenes manufacturer for major tech companies, benefiting from trends like reshoring and increased demand for AI and EV infrastructure. While not a "sexy" stock, Jabil offers a leveraged bet on global tech hardware demand and is considered a long-term conviction play for investors interested in the backbone of technological advancement.

feed.title: ICL Group (NYSE:ICL) Valuation Check After Pivot To Specialty Businesses And Bartek Stake Acquisition
feed.summary: ICL Group (NYSE:ICL) is drawing attention after reporting a 5% rise in annual sales and outlining a pivot towards specialty businesses, including an acquisition of a 49.9% stake in Bartek Ingredients. Despite recent share price weakness, the company is considered 27.0% undervalued by a common narrative, with a fair value estimate of $6.74 compared to its current price of $4.94, driven by expectations of future growth in specialty minerals. However, its current P/E ratio of 28.2x suggests a richer pricing than industry and peer averages, indicating potential valuation risk if growth expectations are not met.

feed.title: Why Park-Ohio (PKOH) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: Park-Ohio (NASDAQ:PKOH) shares dropped significantly after the company reported disappointing fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, missing analysts' estimates for profit and revenue. The negative financial report was exacerbated by a much weaker-than-expected U.S. jobs report, which showed a loss of 92,000 jobs in February, including 12,000 in manufacturing. This combination of poor company performance and negative economic indicators fueled investor concerns, leading to an 11.4% decline in the stock price during the afternoon session.

feed.title: Why WESCO (WCC) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: Shares of WESCO (NYSE:WCC) fell by 5.1% after a much weaker-than-expected U.S. jobs report for February, which indicated an unexpected loss of 92,000 jobs and an increased unemployment rate. This macroeconomic news contributed to a broader market sell-off, particularly impacting industrial stocks. The decline follows a previous drop related to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and rising crude oil prices, both of which raise concerns about inflation and future interest rate decisions by the Federal Reserve.

feed.title: LSB Industries outlines shift to cost-plus sales mix and anticipates higher H2 2025 EBITDA as UAN and AN volumes climb
feed.summary: LSB Industries is shifting its sales strategy towards a higher percentage of cost-plus contracts for its ammonia products. The company projects increased earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) in the second half of 2025, driven by anticipated growth in urea ammonium nitrate (UAN) and ammonium nitrate (AN) volumes. This strategic move aims to stabilize revenues and improve profitability.

feed.title: Titan International (NYSE:TWI) Major Shareholder Sells $27,219,527.60 in Stock
feed.summary: Aipct Holdings LLC, a major shareholder in Titan International (NYSE:TWI), sold 3,041,288 shares for $27,219,527.60 on March 4, 2026, reducing its stake by 25.51%. Following this news, TWI shares fell by approximately 2.4% to $8.68. The company recently missed EPS estimates but exceeded revenue expectations, and it currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average target price of $11.67.

feed.title: Auto Aftermarket: Magna, LKQ see Q4 growth despite headwinds
feed.summary: Magna International and LKQ Corporation both reported increased revenues in the fourth quarter of 2025, despite challenges like softer vehicle production and broader industry headwinds. Magna saw sales grow to US$10.8 billion, while LKQ's revenue increased to US$3.3 billion, though adjusted earnings for both companies faced pressure. Both companies expressed optimism for 2026, with Magna projecting sales between US$41.9 billion and US$43.5 billion, and LKQ providing adjusted earnings per share guidance of US$2.90 to US$3.20.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan Grasberg Deal Extends Copper Growth And Adds Fresh Risks
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan has secured a long-term Memorandum of Understanding with the Indonesian government, extending operating rights at its Grasberg mine. This agreement, along with the phased restart of the Grasberg Block Cave mine and new expansion projects, positions the company to meet growing global copper demand driven by AI and green energy. While the deal reduces regulatory uncertainty, investors should consider the ongoing operational and environmental risks associated with its complex Indonesian operations.

feed.title: TSMC to hire 8,000 workers in 2026
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) announced plans to hire 8,000 new employees in 2026 to support its expansion efforts. The chipmaker is holding recruitment events across Taiwan, including a job fair at National Taiwan University, and is offering an average annual salary of NT$2.2 million to new engineers with master's degrees. TSMC is seeking talent for various technical fields and emerging technologies due to its digital transformation and AI focus.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Linda Dempsey Sells 2,000 Shares of CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF)
feed.summary: Linda Dempsey, Vice President of Public Affairs at CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF), sold 2,000 shares of the company on March 5, 2026. This transaction reduces her total holdings to 17,547 shares. The company's stock was trading at $106.5 per share, making it modestly overvalued according to its GF Value.

feed.title: Distribution Solutions Group Q4 Loss & Annual Profit | 2025 Results - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Distribution Solutions Group, an industrial products manufacturer, reported a net loss of $6.4 million, or 14 cents per share, for its most recent quarter, though adjusted earnings were 18 cents per share. The company's quarterly revenue reached $481.6 million. For the full fiscal year, Distribution Solutions Group achieved a net profit of $8.3 million, or 18 cents per share, with annual revenue nearing $2 billion.

feed.title: ASLE Q4 Deep Dive: Margin Expansion Amid Revenue Shortfall and Demand Shifts
feed.summary: AerSale (ASLE) reported Q4 CY2025 results that missed revenue and adjusted EPS expectations, with sales declining 4% year-on-year. Despite the revenue shortfall attributed to reduced flight equipment sales, the company achieved margin expansion due to growth in recurring segments like component maintenance and USM, alongside efficiency initiatives. Management is focused on expanding its recurring revenue base and leveraging newly added maintenance capacity to drive future performance.

feed.title: Quaker Houghton Reports Fourth-Quarter Revenue Increase in 2025
feed.summary: Quaker Houghton reported a 6% increase in fourth-quarter 2025 net sales, reaching $468.5 million, largely due to acquisitions and currency gains despite weaker market demand. The company's adjusted EBITDA grew by 11% to $71.9 million for the quarter. While the full year saw a net loss of $2.5 million due to impairment and restructuring costs, non-GAAP full-year net income was $123.2 million.

feed.title: Why Kennametal (KMT) Shares Are Getting Obliterated Today
feed.summary: Shares of Kennametal (KMT) fell 7.6% after a weaker-than-expected February jobs report, which showed the U.S. economy unexpectedly shed 92,000 jobs and the manufacturing sector lost 12,000 jobs. This news sparked concerns about an economic slowdown and reduced investor confidence, despite the company's shares having gained 27.5% year-to-date. The decline highlights how market overreactions to economic data can impact even less volatile stocks.

feed.title: Kirby Corp (KEX, US4990491049): Inland Tanker Leader Navigates Rate Cycle and Fed Uncertainty
feed.summary: Kirby Corp (KEX) is a leading North American inland waterway freight and refined products transporter, offering cyclical exposure tied to US industrial production, Gulf Coast energy exports, and Federal Reserve policy. The company operates marine transportation and distribution/services segments, benefiting from a robust domestic infrastructure and high barriers to entry due to the Jones Act. Investors are currently watching inland barge day-rates, the health of its distribution segment, and capital allocation strategies amidst moderating inflation and a cautious Fed.

feed.title: Quanex Building Products (NX) Posts US$489.8 Million Q1 Revenue Yet Trailing Losses Challenge Bull Case
feed.summary: Quanex Building Products (NX) reported Q1 2026 revenue of US$489.8 million and basic EPS of US$0.43, following a mixed period including a Q4 2025 net income and a trailing 12-month net loss of US$250.8 million. While bulls point to strong growth forecasts and a discounted stock price with a DCF fair value significantly higher than its current trading price, critics highlight the company's unprofitability over the last year and issues with interest and dividend coverage. The discrepancy between bullish assumptions for rapid earnings growth and current trailing losses presents a challenge for investors.

feed.title: Kadant (KAI) Margin Compression Tests Premium Valuation Narrative After FY 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: Kadant (KAI) concluded FY 2025 with revenue of US$286.2 million and EPS of US$2.04, bringing its trailing twelve-month figures to US$1.1 billion in revenue and US$8.66 in EPS. Despite analyst expectations for 16.3% earnings growth, trailing EPS and net income have seen a recent decline, with net margins easing to 9.7% from 10.6% a year ago. The stock's current P/E of 40.1x is above its peers and industry, and its DCF fair value of US$301.14 is below the current share price of US$347.31, suggesting a premium valuation that may not fully align with recent performance trends.

feed.title: How Strong February Sales Amid Sector Weakness Will Impact Fastenal (FAST) Investors
feed.summary: Fastenal reported strong February net sales and daily sales growth of 13.3% year-on-year, driven by its on-site service expansion and MRO distribution. Despite this sales momentum, the company faces mixed analyst views and broader sector weakness, leading to a disconnect between its performance and current market sentiment. Investors need to weigh Fastenal's growth thesis and premium valuation against potential pressures from higher costs, elevated inventories, and a challenging industrial distribution sector.

feed.title: Insider Sell: John Roush Sells Shares of Advanced Energy Industr
feed.summary: John Roush, a Director at Advanced Energy Industries Inc (AEIS), sold 10,225 shares of the company on March 4, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 27,593 shares. The company's stock is currently trading at $323.56, giving it a market cap of over $10 billion and a high price-to-earnings ratio, suggesting it is significantly overvalued based on its GuruFocus Value.

feed.title: Michael Clarke Named CEO of Sanmina
feed.summary: Sanmina Corp. has appointed Michael Clarke as its new CEO, effective October 1, 2018, following the resignation of Bob Eulau. Clarke, a board member since 2013, brings over 30 years of executive experience in electronics manufacturing to the role. The company also reaffirmed its strong fiscal fourth-quarter outlook for 2018, expecting revenues between $1.825 to $1.875 billion.

feed.title: Myers Industries 2025 Financial Results: $34.9M Annual Profit - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Myers Industries Inc. reported its financial results for Q4 and the full year 2025, with an annual profit of $34.9 million on $825.7 million in revenue. The company recorded an $11.3 million net income for the fourth quarter. This announcement is set within a broader report on the plastic box industry in the United States, providing market analysis, forecasts to 2035, and insights into demand, supply, and trade dynamics.

feed.title: Methode Electronics Balances Growth Ambitions With Margin Strain
feed.summary: Methode Electronics' recent earnings call revealed a combination of strong cash generation and growth in industrial and data center markets, alongside significant margin pressures. The company is actively rationalizing its portfolio and reducing capital expenditure to protect cash flow, but is facing challenges from North American automotive weakness and operational issues in Mexico, leading to a cut in adjusted EBITDA guidance.

feed.title: News | Blackstone executive says industrial demand ‘poised to accelerate’ as US reindustrializes
feed.summary: Blackstone's global real estate head, Nadeem Meghji, anticipates a significant acceleration in industrial demand, particularly for warehouses and data centers, driven by U.S. reindustrialization and the growing need for AI infrastructure. The firm has substantial holdings in these areas and is exploring options for public investment in data centers. Blackstone reported strong returns in its infrastructure business, emphasizing the potential for continued growth.

feed.title: Emerson Electric's Shares Plunge 2.71% as Daily Trading Volume Dives 48.32% and Ranking Slides From 351st to 472nd
feed.summary: Emerson Electric (EMR) shares experienced a 2.71% decline, with trading volume plummeting by 48.32%, causing its market activity ranking to drop significantly. This occurred despite a recent strong earnings report, suggesting short-term profit-taking or shifting sentiment amidst broader market dynamics. The article discusses Emerson's solid fundamentals and strategic positioning against its recent stock performance pullback due to valuation concerns and regional economic headwinds.

feed.title: How Is W.W. Grainger's Stock Performance Compared to Other Industrial Stocks?
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger (GWW) is a large-cap industrial distributor with a market cap of $54.3 billion, actively integrating AI. The company's stock has rallied 14.1% over the past three months, outperforming the iShares U.S. Industrials ETF (IYJ), but has underperformed IYJ and rival Fastenal (FAST) over the past 52 weeks. Despite recent positive quarterly results, analysts currently hold a "Hold" rating on GWW.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser to Release Third Quarter Results on October 24
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser Company (NYSE: WY) announced it will release its third-quarter 2024 results on Thursday, October 24, after the market closes. The company will host a live webcast and conference call on Friday, October 25, at 7 a.m. Pacific (10 a.m. Eastern) to discuss the results. Weyerhaeuser is a major owner of timberlands and a manufacturer of wood products, operating as a real estate investment trust.

feed.title: Hexcel Reaches Cooperation Deal, Adds Veteran Aerospace Director
feed.summary: Hexcel Corporation (HXL) has entered a cooperation agreement with Vision One Fund, LP, resulting in the appointment of former Kaman Corporation CEO Neal J. Keating to Hexcel's board and audit committee. This agreement, which includes Vision One withdrawing its director nominees and accepting specific obligations, signals a resolution with the activist shareholder. Analysts currently rate HXL stock as a Buy with a $102.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, assesses the stock as Neutral due to a balance of strong financials and cash generation against increased leverage and lower profitability.

feed.title: Harvard Bioscience executes 1-for-10 reverse stock split
feed.summary: Harvard Bioscience (NASDAQ:HBIO) will implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split, effective March 13, 2026, to increase its per-share trading price and maintain Nasdaq listing compliance. The split will reduce outstanding shares from approximately 44.7 million to 4.47 million, with fractional shares being cashed out. Despite recent revenue decline, analysts predict profitability for the company this year.

feed.title: Beyond drops ‘Meat’ from name as it broadens ambitions for plant-based proteins
feed.summary: Beyond Meat is rebranding to Beyond The Plant Protein Co., reflecting its expanded portfolio beyond plant-based meat into other protein categories like beverages. This change comes as the company seeks to meet a wider range of consumer protein needs and diversify its offerings amidst a decline in plant-based meat sales. Despite the name change, Beyond states it remains committed to leadership in plant-based meat.

feed.title: Hooker Furnishings declares $0.115 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Hooker Furnishings Corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.115 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The company, which has maintained dividend payments for 26 consecutive years, offers a dividend yield of 3.37%. Despite revenue challenges and a recent significant earnings miss, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock appears undervalued, and the company is also expanding its board through a cooperation agreement with Global Value Investment Corporation.

feed.title: Toro’s Red Iron originations dip 1.1% YoY
feed.summary: The Toro Co. reported a 1.1% year-over-year dip in dealer and distributor receivables financed through its Red Iron Acceptance joint venture, totaling $546.8 million in Q1. Outstanding receivables also fell by 17% YoY to $797.4 million. Despite these shifts, Toro maintains its financing presence and relies on other third-party institutions for additional inventory financing.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin said it is quadrupling munitions production
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin announced it is quadrupling the production of "critical munitions" in response to the United States and Israel's attack on Iran, a ramp-up that began months ago. President Donald Trump later met with top U.S. defense manufacturing companies to discuss this expansion, noting that plants and production for these weapons are already underway. This comes after a January agreement with the Pentagon to triple PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement interceptor production, reflecting a soaring global demand.

feed.title: Kathleen A. Walters Joins Jabil Board of Directors
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. announced that Kathleen A. Walters has been appointed to its Board of Directors. Walters previously served on Jabil's board from 2005-2011 and brings extensive operating experience from her executive roles at Georgia-Pacific Corp, Sappi Fine Paper North America, Kimberly‐Clark Corporation, and Scott Paper Company. Both Jabil's chairman, Tim Main, and vice chairman, Tom Sansone, expressed confidence that her background in manufacturing and consumer products, along with her familiarity with Jabil, will be a valuable asset to the company's continued success.

feed.title: Technical Reactions to CMCO Trends in Macro Strategies
feed.summary: Quantitative Research Desk reports on Columbus McKinnon Corporation (NASDAQ: CMCO), indicating weak near and mid-term sentiment but a strong long-term positive outlook. The analysis highlights an exceptional 49.0:1 risk-reward setup targeting a 15.3% gain against 0.3% risk. Three distinct AI-generated trading strategies are provided for different risk profiles, including position trading, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis.

feed.title: 200 Texas workers will be laid off at Campbell’s plant as famed brand moves from producing soups to sauces
feed.summary: Campbell's will lay off approximately 200 workers at its Paris, Texas plant as it shifts production from soups to sauces like Prego and Pace salsa. This move finalizes a decision announced in July 2024 to转型 the facility into a flagship sauce plant. Although the change impacts fewer roles than initially anticipated, the company is also closing its Cape Cod chips plant in Massachusetts, leading to nearly 50 additional job losses.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Director Reyne Wisecup Sells 36,920 Shares
feed.summary: Fastenal Director Reyne Wisecup sold 36,920 shares of the company's stock on March 5th for approximately $1.75 million, reducing her position by 48%. The sale leaves her with 40,000 shares. This transaction follows Fastenal's recent quarterly earnings report, where it met consensus EPS estimates and announced an increase in its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Boeing reportedly close to 500-jet order at US-China summit
feed.summary: Boeing is expected to secure an order for 500 737 Max jets during President Trump's upcoming visit to China. Additionally, the United States and China are in discussions for orders of approximately 100 787 Dreamliners and 777X planes. This potential deal highlights significant aerospace trade between the two nations.

feed.title: Sonoco to increase uncoated recycled paperboard prices by $70 per ton
feed.summary: Sonoco Products Company announced a $70 per ton price increase for all grades of uncoated recycled paperboard in the United States and Canada, effective April 3, 2026. This increase is due to tightening market conditions, higher mill utilization, and increased input costs. Additionally, converted paperboard products, including tubes and protective packaging, will see an 8% price increase starting April 15, 2026.

feed.title: Greenwave Technology Solutions Q3: $12.68M revenue, $(5.28M) net loss — 10-Q summary
feed.summary: Greenwave Technology Solutions reported Q3 2025 revenue of $12.68 million and a net loss of $(5.28) million, attributed to expanded metal recycling operations, increased hauling volumes, and strategic channel shifts. The company highlighted its transition to metal recycling, operational capacity expansion with two automotive shredders, and moves to broaden market access. These efforts have supported revenue growth but also led to increased operating costs and integration expenses.

feed.title: Gas and Liquid Handling Stocks Fourth Quarter Summary: Comparing Gorman-Rupp (NYSE:GRC)

feed.title: Toyota Recalls 550,000 Highlander, Highlander Hybrid SUVs for Seat Issue
feed.summary: Toyota is recalling approximately 550,000 Highlander and Highlander Hybrid SUVs from model years 2021-2024 due to an issue with the second-row seat recliner assemblies. These assemblies may not fully lock after adjustment, posing an injury risk in a high-speed crash. Toyota will replace the return springs in the recliner assemblies for free, and owners will be notified by early May.

feed.title: Has Chart Industries (GTLS) Price Stalled While Long Term Returns Reflect Further Potential Value?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Chart Industries (GTLS) is still a valuable investment, despite its recent share price stagnation, by examining its long-term returns and valuation. Using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and Price/Sales (P/S) ratio, the analysis suggests GTLS may be undervalued. The article also introduces "Narratives" as a way for investors to customize their valuation models based on their own assumptions about the company's future.

feed.title: ArcelorMittal plans higher $0.60 dividend, buybacks with key shareholder
feed.summary: ArcelorMittal filed its 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F, highlighting its disciplined capital allocation with $1.1 billion in strategic capital expenditure and $0.7 billion returned to shareholders. The company proposed a higher dividend of $0.60 per share for FY2026 and confirmed a policy to return at least 50% of post-dividend free cash flow to shareholders via buybacks. A significant shareholder, nearing a 45% stake threshold, has also agreed to participate in the share repurchase program.

feed.title: ArcelorMittal announces the publication of its annual report 2025 on Form 20 F and the publication of its 2025 annual report

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) Shares Down 4.4% - Here's Why

While long-term bookings from Broadcom and accelerated fab expansion support future growth, concerns around export controls and Nvidia's capacity reallocation for China-bound products introduce near-term uncertainty. Despite strong fundamentals and generally bullish analyst ratings, these factors contributed to the stock's decline.

feed.title: Walmart: Calling all entrepreneurs

The program includes "Tour Stop" pitch meetings in various cities, leading up to Open Call 2026 in Bentonville in October for successful applicants. This initiative supports Walmart's commitment to US manufacturing, aiming to sell $350 billion in US-made goods by 2030 and support over 750,000 jobs.

feed.title: How Novanta’s 2025 Results and 2026 Revenue Outlook Will Impact Novanta (NOVT) Investors

feed.title: Economic implications of war: Middle East war and the automotive industry
feed.summary: This article analyzes the potential economic impact of the ongoing war in the Middle East on the automotive industry, categorizing the effects based on the conflict's duration: short-term, midterm, and longer-term scenarios. It details how oil prices, shipping costs, vehicle production, and consumer demand for different vehicle types would be affected. S&P Global Mobility currently maintains a base-case presumption of a short-term conflict for its vehicle production and sales forecasts, but acknowledges the significant uncertainties.

feed.title: Generac Expands Into AI Data Center Power With EPC Partnership

This collaboration aims to provide comprehensive power systems for hyperscale facilities by combining Generac's battery systems with EPC Power's controllers and inverters. The strategic shift could be a significant growth driver, although it introduces new execution risks and challenges to Generac's existing business narrative.

feed.title: Dow Inc. (DOW) Stock Moves -1.30%: What You Should Know

feed.title: Honda Recalls 65,000 SUVs For Screens That May Go Black
feed.summary: Honda is recalling 65,135 electric crossovers, including the 2024 Honda Prologue and 2024 Acura ZDX, due to software defects that can cause instrument and infotainment displays to go blank. This issue prevents drivers from seeing critical information like speed and warning lights. The recall affects vehicles built on General Motors' Ultium battery platform, and Honda will instruct owners to take their vehicles to a service center for a software update.

feed.title: Barclays Boosts CF Industries Price Target Amid Geopolitical Tensions
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for CF Industries (CF) from $100 to $120, maintaining an Overweight rating due to anticipated elevated nitrogen prices caused by geopolitical tensions. Other firms like Goldman Sachs and RBC Capital have also raised their targets, highlighting the company's strong financial health and the ongoing global nitrogen market tightness. CF Industries is well-positioned to benefit from these market dynamics and its strategic focus on low-carbon ammonia projects.

feed.title: Greif Named 2026 Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award Winner for a Third Time
feed.summary: Greif (NYSE: GEF, GEF.B) has been awarded the 2026 Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award (GEWA) for the third consecutive year, also earning the "Winner With Distinction" honor. This recognition highlights Greif's commitment to fostering a highly engaged workplace culture, which Gallup's research links to superior business outcomes in various areas. The company attributes this success to its global colleagues' dedication to accountability, engagement, and teamwork, reinforcing its core values and creating lasting value for stakeholders.

feed.title: Coherent Announces General Availability of Industry's First Dual-Laser QSFP28-DCO for Single Fiber Transmission
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. has announced the general availability of its new dual-laser QSFP28-DCO module, which enables bi-directional 100G coherent transmission over a single fiber. This innovation addresses network constraints by allowing increased capacity and extended reach up to 300km in fiber-limited environments. The solution uses a proven technology platform and aims to provide greater flexibility and scalability for network operators.

feed.title: State sues Cleveland-Cliffs, Duck Creek over handling of radioactive waste
feed.summary: The state of Ohio has filed a lawsuit against Cleveland-Cliffs and Duck Creek, alleging improper handling of radioactive waste. The litigation concerns the Cleveland-Cliffs Cleveland Works steel mill facility. The lawsuit was filed on March 6, 2026.

feed.title: Orion S.A. Announces its 2026 Annual General Meeting Date and Record Date
feed.summary: Orion S.A. (NYSE: OEC), a global specialty chemicals company, has announced its 2026 annual general meeting of shareholders will take place on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at 2 p.m. Central European Time in Luxembourg. The company also set its record date for shareholder admission to the meeting as April 23, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. Central European Time. The meeting will be held in person and streamed live online, allowing for virtual attendance and remote exercise of shareholder rights.

feed.title: Amprius Technologies 10-K: Revenue $73.0M, EPS $(0.35)
feed.summary: Amprius Technologies reported strong financial performance in fiscal 2025, with revenue soaring 202% to $73.0 million, driven by increased SiCore battery sales. The company significantly improved its net loss per share to $(0.35) and achieved a positive gross profit of $8.3 million, marking a transition to commercial ramp-up and a strategic shift to contract manufacturing for scalable production. Amprius also expanded its pilot line and launched its 100% silicon anode SiCore product, targeting aviation, LEV, and EV markets.

feed.title: Koppers" Q4 adjusted EBITDA falls 4% on market share shift and competitive pressures

Despite this, the company saw a 9.7% rise in domestic utility pole volumes and anticipates an adjusted EPS of $4.20 to $5.00 for 2026, driven by strategic initiatives and cost reductions including idling two facilities. The net income for Q4 2025 was $29.7 million, a significant improvement from a net loss in the prior year.

feed.title: Dow rallies nearly 300 points on gains for Amazon.com Inc., Caterpillar stocks

feed.title: Boeing Secures Major Orders Amid Skyrocketing Growth in Aircraft Demand
feed.summary: Boeing has secured significant new orders and is experiencing optimism in the market, with its stock trading up 3.85%. feed.title: Cal-Maine Foods Buys Creighton Brothers and Crystal Lake Assets for $128.5M

feed.title: Terex appoints Joseph LaDue as chief accounting officer after REV Group merger

This appointment follows Stephen Johnston's departure, which the company states was not due to any disagreement.

feed.title: Terex (NYSE: TEX) taps REV executive as new chief accounting officer
feed.summary: Terex Corporation has announced a change in its finance leadership following its merger with REV Group, Inc. Stephen Johnston has stepped down as Vice President, Chief Accounting Officer and Controller, effective March 2, 2026, and will be temporarily retained for a smooth transition. Joseph LaDue, previously from REV Group, has been appointed as the new VP, Chief Accounting Officer and Controller, with a base salary of $357,500 and eligibility for significant incentive bonuses and long-term awards.

feed.title: Bluejay 10-K: Net loss $6.85M, operating loss $6.95M; cash through Q3 2026

Business highlights include progress on the Symphony rapid blood diagnostics platform, focusing on an ICU/ER sepsis application with a planned FDA submission, and scaling manufacturing.

(ZENA) is integrating drone-based workflows into land surveying for U.S. homebuilders to significantly reduce permit-ready survey times from up to ten days to three or fewer. By leveraging its acquisition of Cardinal Civil Resources and its Drone as a Service (DaaS) network, ZenaTech is expanding into the trillion-dollar U.S. homebuilding market. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, views ZENA as Neutral, citing weak profitability and challenged cash flow despite strong revenue growth and a strong uptrend in technicals.

feed.title: New Coherent module squeezes 10x more data through a single fiber line

The solution aims to maximize fiber plant utilization for Cable/MSO and wireless transport applications.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Demands Rise as AI-Ready Consumer Devices Surge in Asia

This trend, driven by a shift in consumer behavior towards AI-capable hardware, has led to a significant boost in earnings and orders for these companies. Key regions like Taiwan, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, and Malaysia are adapting to this demand by expanding production, improving supply chains, and investing in specialized AI-specific chip components.

feed.title: Quanex Building Products 10-Q: Revenue $409.1M, EPS loss $(0.09)
feed.summary: Quanex Building Products reported quarterly net sales of $409.1 million, an increase of 2% year-over-year, and a basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.09), which is an improvement compared to the prior year. Management highlighted continued investments in product engineering and the potential for future M&A activities to support growth.

feed.title: Trump Says Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Others To Quadruple Advanced Weapons Output Amid Iran War
feed.summary: Donald Trump announced that major U.S. defense companies, including Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Raytheon, have agreed to quadruple production of "exquisite class" weaponry amid the ongoing conflict with Iran. This move follows a meeting with CEOs and is intended to rapidly increase weapon quantity, with manufacturing already underway. Meanwhile, Chief Economic Advisor Mohamed El-Erian warned about the significant risks the prolonged U.S.-Israel-Iran war poses to the global economy, particularly concerning supply chains and potential inflation.

feed.title: J.P. Morgan Upgrades Dow Inc. (DOW) Amid Rising Oil Prices

feed.title: Boeing Co Stock (BA) Moved Up by 3.70% on Mar 6: Facts Behind the Movement

Technical indicators show mixed signals, with a sell signal from MACD but neutral to oversold conditions from RSI and Williams %R.

feed.title: Eaton (ETN) Appoints David Foster as EVP and CFO Following 2025 Financial Results

His extensive background in finance within Eaton and deep understanding of its operations were highlighted as key assets for the company's next phase of global power management business.

feed.title: Daily 5 report for March 6: Ford’s Plan B for Kentucky battery plant

The report also highlights other automotive news including disruptions from the Iran war, dealership buy-sell updates, and GM's leadership changes.

feed.title: Hurco Companies Q1: Revenue $42.9M, EPS $(0.54)

feed.title: Matson, BNSF, War-Lok Team on Cargo Security

The program, starting in Q2 2026, will utilize War-Lok security devices on international containers moving from Los Angeles to BNSF network destinations and position Matson containers in the lower well of intermodal railcars for added protection, all at no extra cost to customers. This initiative seeks to significantly reduce theft exposure for Matson's customers.

feed.title: Lantronix Expands Embedded Compute Platform With MediaTek-Based SOM Solutions
feed.summary: Lantronix Inc. has announced a significant expansion of its embedded compute platform with new System-on-Module (SOM) solutions based on MediaTek Inc.'s Genio family of System-on-Chip (SOC) platforms. This strategic move aims to broaden Lantronix’s solutions portfolio, increase market coverage for Edge AI deployments, and strengthen supply chain resiliency. The MediaTek-based SOMs will address high-volume, power-efficient Edge AI deployments and support the NVIDIA TAO framework, with a drone technology demonstration planned for Embedded World 2026.

feed.title: Why Methode Electronics (MEI) Stock Is Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: Shares of Methode Electronics (MEI) fell significantly after the company reported weak fourth-quarter results, missed earnings estimates, and issued a disappointing full-year profitability forecast. Although net sales beat expectations, adjusted loss per share and adjusted EBITDA were below analyst projections. This poor performance and weaker financial outlook overshadowed a previous stock gain related to the U.S. Supreme Court striking down certain tariffs.

feed.title: TGLS: B. Riley Securities Lowers Price Target to $65.00, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: B. Riley Securities has lowered its price target for Tecnoglass (TGLS) to $65.00 from $70.00, while still maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment follows similar moves by DA Davidson, which also reduced its price targets for TGLS in recent months. Despite these reductions, the average target price from four analysts for TGLS remains at $66.25, indicating a significant upside from its current trading price, and the stock holds an "Outperform" consensus rating.

feed.title: VULCAN DECLARES QUARTERLY DIVIDEND ON COMMON STOCK
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 cents per share on its common stock. The dividend is payable on September 6, 2023, to shareholders of record as of August 17, 2023. Vulcan is the largest producer of construction aggregates in the U.S.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley reiterates Overweight on Sherwin-Williams stock
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reiterated an Overweight rating on Sherwin-Williams Co. with a price target of $385.00, despite the stock trading near its 52-week low and InvestingPro suggesting it may be overvalued. The firm maintains a positive outlook on the company due to strong earnings power, best-in-class pricing power, and consistent dividend increases, noting accelerating market share growth and conservative future guidance. Recent financial results for Q4 2025 showed Sherwin-Williams surpassed analyst expectations with EPS of $2.23 and revenue of $5.6 billion, and the company extended a $75 million credit agreement to boost financial flexibility.

feed.title: PDC Brands, Church & Dwight, Mars, Lowe's Execs to Discuss Intelligent Supply Chains at Analytics Unite
feed.summary: Executives from PDC Brands, Church & Dwight, Mars, and Lowe's will participate in a panel discussion at Analytics Unite 2026 to discuss intelligent supply chains. They will explore how autonomous and AI-enabled technologies can reduce risk, improve efficiency, and foster growth amid market volatility. The event, taking place in Chicago from April 7-9, will focus on leveraging advanced analytics to manage fluctuating demand and global disruptions.

feed.title: Ford makes big shift into energy storage to keep battery plants busy
feed.summary: Ford is entering the battery energy storage system business, called Ford Energy, to repurpose a Kentucky factory originally planned for EV battery production, due to slower-than-expected electric vehicle sales. The company will produce large-scale energy storage units for utility companies and data centers, facing competition from established players like Tesla. Ford believes its manufacturing expertise and licensing of CATL technology give it an advantage in this new venture.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Raises Caterpillar (CAT) Price Target to $817 | CAT Stock News
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for Caterpillar (CAT) from $729.00 to $817.00, maintaining an "Outperform" rating. feed.title: NN (NASDAQ:NNBR) Price Target Lowered to $3.00 at B. Riley Financial

This adjustment follows NN's recent earnings miss, with the company reporting a loss of $0.29 per share compared to an expected $0.02, causing a 6.9% stock drop to $1.35. The company faces negative margins, a high debt-to-equity ratio of 3.01, and a mixed analyst outlook, with institutional investors holding approximately 54% of its shares.

feed.title: Astronics (OTCMKTS:ATROB) Stock Price Down 15.1% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Astronics (OTCMKTS:ATROB) stock dropped 15.1% to $70.49 on Friday, with trading volume significantly below its daily average despite the company beating quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. feed.title: Socket Mobile announces iPhone 17e scanner compatibility
feed.summary: Socket Mobile Inc. announced that its XtremeScan, DuraSled, and DuraScan Terminal scanning products are now compatible with Apple’s new iPhone 17e. The company, facing financial challenges including a ~21% stock decline over the past year and decreasing revenue, aims to support businesses deploying iOS-based data capture systems in industrial and commercial settings. The announcement highlights Socket Mobile's continued alignment with Apple and its focus on providing durable and reliable industrial scanning solutions through its CaptureSDK software.

feed.title: Can AI Initiatives & Improving Margins Drive Cognex Shares in 2026?
feed.summary: Cognex (CGNX) is strategically integrating AI into its industrial machine vision and optimizing its cost structure, leading to improved profitability and cash generation. The company raised its through-cycle adjusted EBITDA target to 25-31% for 2026, driven by operational expense reductions and portfolio optimization. Despite a premium valuation (P/E of 39.39), Cognex maintains a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) due to upward earnings estimate revisions and expects mid to high-single-digit organic revenue growth across various verticals, supported by a strong balance sheet with no debt.

feed.title: 9 Stocks Outperforming the Market
feed.summary: Energy, materials, and industrial stocks have significantly outpaced the broader market since October, largely due to strong performances from a select group of nine companies. These gains are driven by diverse factors including surging power demand, commodity price strength, and geopolitical developments. While some companies benefit from rising data center electricity demand, others are supported by long-standing business fundamentals and investment expectations.

feed.title: Regal Rexnord Introduces Falk BK & BF Gear Drives With TA Bushing Shaft Mounting System
feed.summary: Regal Rexnord has introduced its Falk BK & BF Series Gear Drives with the Torque-Assist (TA) Tapered Bushing System, an expansion designed to enhance reliability and performance across various applications. This new system significantly reduces assembly and disassembly times by up to 50%, improving uptime and ensuring durability in demanding environments like grain and bulk material handling. The TA Bushing System also offers guaranteed removal and a full torque rating, making maintenance easier and more cost-effective.

feed.title: Stratasys Q4 Earnings Beat Estimates, Revenues Slip Y/Y, Shares Fall
feed.summary: Stratasys (SSYS) reported fourth-quarter 2025 non-GAAP earnings of 7 cents per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 40% but declining 41.7% year over year. While revenues fell 7% year over year to $140 million, they still beat the consensus mark. The company's shares dropped over 4.8% following the announcement, although Stratasys provided a positive outlook for 2026, anticipating revenue growth and improved margins.

feed.title: New U.S. Smelter JV and Reporting Shift Might Change The Case For Investing In Century Aluminum (CENX)

These developments are reshaping the company's production strategy and reporting, influencing its investment narrative which heavily relies on expanding U.S. primary aluminum production and managing energy exposure. The article suggests investors consider these strategic shifts and the inherent risks of such large projects when evaluating CENX's future prospects.

feed.title: Century Aluminum 10 K Delay Raises Questions Around New Smelter Plans
feed.summary: Century Aluminum (CENX) announced a delay in filing its 10-K report shortly after significant executive insider stock sales and a new partnership to build a domestic aluminum smelter. This convergence of events raises investor questions regarding transparency, capital needs, and the company's ability to execute its ambitious plans. The delay follows a year of sharply lower net income and occurs amidst a major capital program, prompting scrutiny of the company's financial health and governance.

feed.title: Eastman Kodak honors employee contribution advancing innovation and manufacturing

feed.title: Russ Meyer: A Legacy That Shaped General Aviation
feed.summary: Textron Inc. announced the passing of Russell W. “Russ” Meyer Jr., former chairman & CEO of Cessna Aircraft Company. Meyer was instrumental in the development of the Citation family of business jets and is remembered for his principled leadership and significant impact on the general aviation industry. Tributes from current Textron leadership highlight his vision, integrity, and lasting contributions to both Cessna and the broader American piston aircraft sector.

feed.title: Aquifer rebounds after International Paper closure
feed.summary: The Floridan aquifer in Savannah has significantly rebounded after International Paper closed its mill, leading to a reduction of 10 million gallons per day in water withdrawal. Water levels in some monitoring wells have risen over 19-23 feet, surpassing levels recorded since the 1950s. The Georgia Environmental Protection Division expected this recovery, which is now stabilizing, and is working to protect the aquifer for future use.

(FIX) is strategically positioned to benefit from the data center boom through its modular construction capabilities, which represented 18% of its 2025 revenue. The company plans to expand its modular footprint by 1 million square feet by the end of 2026 and is investing in automation to improve productivity. While strong bookings indicate healthy demand, the extended duration of these projects means revenue conversion may be slower, with significant payoff expected in 2027-2028 rather than the immediate future.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Slashes 200 Jobs at Paris Plant in Sauce Shift Shakeup
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. is cutting over 200 jobs at its Paris, Texas, plant as part of a multi-year retooling to transition the facility from soup production to a flagship sauce plant. This move follows an earlier announcement in July 2024 to wind down soup operations and convert the site for Prego and Pace sauces, affecting a significant portion of the workforce. Local officials and workforce agencies are expected to provide transition and retraining support for the affected employees.

feed.title: Rail News - Rail supplier news from RailPros and Matson. For Railroad Career Professionals

Meanwhile, Matson Inc. is enhancing its cargo security program by partnering with BNSF Railway and War-Lok, adding two layers of protection to international intermodal cargo moving from Los Angeles to BNSF network destinations and beyond, at no additional cost to customers. This initiative, starting in Q2 2026, includes deploying War-Lok security devices on international containers and strategically positioning Matson containers in the lower well of intermodal rail cars.

The SoHo store is designed to resemble a lived-in home, showcasing GreenRow's full product range and a curated selection of art and vintage pieces, aiming to offer customers an immersive experience.

feed.title: General Mills removes certified colors from all school meal items
feed.summary: General Mills has successfully removed all certified colors from its entire portfolio of K-12 school meal items, achieving this goal ahead of its summer 2026 commitment. This move, which involved reformulating Lucky Charms 25% less sugar cereal to reach 100% compliance, aligns with the company's broader objective to remove certified colors from its US cereal portfolio by summer 2026 and its full US retail portfolio by the end of 2027, spurred by the FDA's intention to phase out petroleum-based synthetic food dyes.

feed.title: Nearly 890,000 Ford, Lincoln SUVs Recalled for Backup Camera Images

Affected models include certain Ford Escapes, Lincoln Corsairs, Ford Explorers, and Lincoln Aviators from model years 2020-2024. The remedy is still being developed, with interim owner notifications expected in April and a final fix anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Cleveland-Cliffs Illegally Handled Oil, Gas Waste, Ohio AG Says

This state legal action follows a previous lawsuit by Duck Creek Energy Inc. and a sister company against Cleveland-Cliffs, alleging breach of lease and illegal demolition that resulted in environmental damage. The attorney general's suit aims to hold both Cleveland-Cliffs and Duck Creek responsible for the alleged violations.

feed.title: Mahindra Group and Johnson Controls Launch Net Zero Buildings Initiative to Decarbonize Buildings in India
feed.summary: The Mahindra Group and Johnson Controls have partnered to launch a Net Zero Buildings Initiative in India, aimed at decarbonizing commercial, urban residential, and public buildings. This initiative will provide resources and training to building owners and facility managers, helping them implement sustainable practices and achieve net-zero goals. The program, which includes a toolkit and year-long roadshow, seeks to accelerate the adoption of smart and sustainable building technologies to support India's climate objectives, given that three-quarters of its anticipated 2040 buildings are yet to be constructed.

feed.title: SMR Cooperation Gains Full Momentum in Thailand

The seminar, attended by experts from both countries, focused on SMR deployment conditions in Thailand, technical characteristics of innovative SMRs (i-SMR), and supply chain considerations. This collaboration is expected to advance Thailand's energy transition and expand SMR business in the ASEAN region.

feed.title: Has Elbit Systems UK been involved in the manufacture and supply of weapons to the Israeli military - a claim the company strongly denies?
feed.summary: This article examines the contentious debate surrounding Elbit Systems UK's alleged involvement in supplying military technology to the Israeli armed forces, a claim the company consistently denies. It highlights arguments from activists and investigative journalists based on export licenses and shipping records, suggesting UK-produced components may enter Israeli military systems. The article concludes that while a direct supply of complete weapons systems by Elbit Systems UK is not conclusively proven, there's a documented connection to supply networks linked to Israeli military equipment.

feed.title: Brazil Antitrust Regulator Clears Sale of Braskem Shares to IG4
feed.summary: Brazil's antitrust authority has approved the sale of a controlling stake in petrochemicals producer Braskem SA to asset manager IG4 Capital.
