feed.title: Autodesk’s model for building an AI ‘moat’
feed.summary: Autodesk CEO Andrew Anagnost explains how his company is building an AI "moat" by focusing on high-stakes industries where precision is critical, complex contexts, and proprietary data combined with expert understanding. He notes that the company is shifting towards consumption-based and outcome-based pricing models to adapt to potential AI-driven headcount reductions among clients. Anagnost believes this strategy, combined with their long-term AI investments, will enable Autodesk and its customers to thrive in the evolving AI landscape.

feed.title: An oat campus: Oatly invests $16 million in its Swedish production plant to support growing global demand
feed.summary: Oatly Group AB is investing $16 million in its Landskrona production facility in Sweden to increase capacity by over 33%, from 150 to 200 million liters per year. This expansion aims to meet the accelerating global demand for its plant-based drinks and further reduce the company's climate impact. The project, which will begin in March 2026 and conclude in March 2027, will also lead to increased sourcing of oats from Swedish farmers and further boost exports to key and expansion markets.

feed.title: NVIDIA, Coherent strike multiyear optics agreement

feed.title: First Solar (FSLR): The AI-Driven Evolution of a Renewable Giant
feed.summary: First Solar (FSLR) has transformed from a domestic manufacturing policy play into a critical infrastructure provider for the AI revolution, marked by a significant stock surge in mid-2024. The company, the largest solar manufacturer in the Western Hemisphere, leverages its unique Cadmium Telluride thin-film technology and vertically integrated business model, benefiting from robust demand driven by AI data centers and U.S. domestic content incentives. Despite its strengths, FSLR faces risks from policy dependency and grid interconnection delays, but its expansion into India and innovation in Perovskite tandem cells present significant growth opportunities.

feed.title: Solid Waste Management Business Report 2026: $405+ Bn
feed.summary: The global solid waste management market is projected to grow from $328.9 billion in 2025 to $344.12 billion in 2026, reaching $405.2 billion by 2030, driven by urbanization, increasing waste generation, government initiatives, and environmental concerns. Key trends include organized waste collection, recycling, resource recovery, and waste-to-energy solutions, with Asia-Pacific expected to be the fastest-growing region. Leading companies are adopting technological advancements like waste sorting robots to enhance segregation and recycling efficiencies.

feed.title: E-Waste Management Business Report 2026: $149.96 Bn Market

feed.summary: This article highlights several companies whose stocks made significant moves during the week. C3.ai rose due to its position as a defense contractor, while ADT fell after mixed Q4 2025 results. Xerox gained after expanding its market approach, Corning increased with the launch of a new product, and KLA Corporation fell amidst a sector-wide sell-off.

feed.title: A Look At Illinois Tool Works (ITW) Valuation After Recent Mixed Short Term Returns
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has shown mixed short-term returns but strong long-term gains, leading to investor interest. While analysts generally consider the stock around 1% overvalued with a fair value estimate of $278.63 due to expected margin expansion and operational flexibility, a discounted cash flow model suggests it is significantly overvalued at $170.20 per share. Investors are encouraged to weigh both perspectives, considering potential risks like organic revenue softness and pressures in certain segments.

feed.title: Innoviz Tech (INVZ) Collaborates with Dataspeed to Integrate LiDAR Sensors into Drive-by-Wire Platforms
feed.summary: Innoviz Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ: INVZ) has partnered with Dataspeed Inc. to integrate its InnovizSMART LiDAR sensors into Dataspeed’s drive-by-wire vehicle platforms. This collaboration aims to provide resilient sensing solutions for demanding autonomous research and deployment across various industries in North America, ensuring reliable performance even in challenging environmental conditions. The partnership will deliver purpose-built autonomous vehicles equipped with advanced integrated systems.

feed.title: American Century Companies Inc. Has $50.61 Million Holdings in UFP Technologies, Inc. $UFPT
feed.summary: American Century Companies Inc. has reduced its stake in UFP Technologies, Inc. by 22.0% in the third quarter, now holding 253,532 shares valued at approximately $50.605 million. Despite this, institutional ownership remains high at 87.28%, with other funds like First National Bank of Omaha initiating new positions. UFP Technologies recently beat EPS estimates but narrowly missed revenue forecasts, with analysts currently giving the stock a "Hold" rating and an average price target of $289.00.

feed.title: American Century Companies Inc. Reduces Stock Position in Applied Industrial Technologies, Inc. $AIT
feed.summary: American Century Companies Inc. reduced its stake in Applied Industrial Technologies, Inc. (NYSE:AIT) by 6.5% in the third quarter, selling 13,530 shares and holding 194,438 shares valued at $50.76 million. Despite this, other institutional investors increased their positions, and hedge funds collectively own 93.52% of the company's stock. Applied Industrial Technologies recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.51 and analysts largely view the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $294.38.

feed.title: Are Enovix’s (ENVX) Improving Results And Revenue Guidance Reframing Its Path To Profitability?
feed.summary: Enovix Corporation recently reported improved Q4 2025 sales of US$11.27 million and reduced net losses, alongside full-year 2025 sales of US$31.82 million. The company also provided Q1 2026 revenue guidance of US$6.5 million to US$7.5 million, offering more clarity on its commercial progress. This new information impacts the investment narrative, which hinges on Enovix's ability to scale high-volume production of its silicon batteries and convert qualifications into sustained, higher-margin orders.

feed.title: American Century Companies Inc. Cuts Stock Holdings in Mettler-Toledo International, Inc. $MTD
feed.summary: American Century Companies Inc. significantly reduced its stake in Mettler-Toledo International, Inc. by 45.5% in the third quarter, selling 39,795 shares. Despite this, other major investors like Huntington National Bank and Handelsbanken Fonder AB increased their holdings. Mettler-Toledo International reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and has received mixed analyst ratings with an average "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Navy Awards Lockheed $133M Contract Modification for F-35 Flight Test Support
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has been awarded a $132.9 million contract modification by the U.S. Navy to continue providing flight test support for the F-35 Lightning II aircraft. This modification extends a previous cost-plus-incentive-fee contract for ongoing testing of new F-35 capabilities for the Air Force, Navy, Marine Corps, and non-DOW participants. The work, expected to conclude in March 2027, includes administrative support, maintenance, and test preparation for facilities evaluating new aircraft features, particularly focusing on training systems, flight test labs, ALIS, and ODIN laboratories.

feed.title: L3Harris Charts New Course with Leadership and Portfolio Overhaul
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies is undertaking a major strategic realignment, including executive team changes and the planned spin-off of its missile propulsion division through an IPO. This move aims to enhance the company's focus on defense capabilities and boost value in a high-growth sector. New financial leadership and the appointment of a head for the Missile Solutions segment are critical steps to prepare the unit for its future as an independent public entity.

feed.title: How Genuine Parts to split operations into Automotive, Industrial companies
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Company (GPC) will separate its operations into two independent, publicly traded entities, Global Automotive and Global Industrial, by the first quarter of 2027. This strategic move aims to unlock long-term shareholder value and enhance operational clarity, with each new company focusing on distinct market opportunities and investment strategies. The Global Automotive business will focus on the "do it for me" (DIFM) market, while Global Industrial will target consolidation in the fragmented industrial distribution landscape.

feed.title: Deere & Company Stock: Navigating the Agricultural Downturn
feed.summary: Deere & Company is facing a challenging agricultural downturn due to declining commodity prices and high operational costs, with 2026 anticipated as the market trough for large machinery. The company is responding by pushing advanced technology, such as new tractor series and enhanced seeding systems, to drive future growth. Despite the market headwinds, Deere's diversification through its construction and forestry division and commitment to shareholder returns, including a quarterly dividend, are providing support, with the stock showing a significant year-to-date gain.

feed.title: Belden Enables Safer, Smarter IT/OT Networks with Virtual Firewall and Digital I/O Module Innovations
feed.summary: Belden has launched new products to enhance IT/OT network security and efficiency, including a next-generation virtual firewall and their first-ever Digital I/O Modules under the Belden brand. The virtual firewall offers flexible, scalable cybersecurity for operational technology environments, while the new I/O modules aim to reduce system costs and improve efficiency. These innovations represent significant advancements in Belden's portfolio of connection solutions.

feed.title: Zentek Ltd (ZTEK, ISIN CA98936C1068) under the microscope: What international investors should know
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth look at Zentek Ltd (ZTEK, ISIN CA98936C1068), a speculative nanotechnology and advanced materials company, for international investors. It covers the company's business model, technology focus on graphene-based solutions, and strategic positioning in the advanced materials sector. The analysis emphasizes the importance of commercial validation, partnerships, regulatory transparency, and the influence of macroeconomic factors like central bank policies on high-risk innovation stocks.

feed.title: Broadwind Energy, Inc. (BWEN) Sees a More Significant Dip Than Broader Market: Some Facts to Know
feed.summary: Broadwind Energy, Inc. (BWEN) experienced a significant dip of -4.45% in its latest trading session, underperforming the broader market. The stock has fallen 24.23% over the past month, while the Industrial Products sector gained 0.8%. Investors are awaiting the upcoming earnings report on March 11, 2026, with EPS projected at -$0.05 and revenue at $36.97 million. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell), reflecting recent negative estimate revisions.

feed.title: NIO Inc. marks production milestone of 2 million e-drive units
feed.summary: NIO Inc. has announced a significant manufacturing milestone, with its two-millionth electric drive unit rolling off the production line at its second plant in Hefei. This achievement highlights NIO's robust in-house capabilities in electric powertrain production and rapid scaling of its core drivetrain manufacturing. The milestone unit is destined for the latest generation NIO ES8, which also recently surpassed 70,000 cumulative deliveries.

feed.title: Vector and Microchip strengthen collaboration to deliver pre‑integrated embedded solutions
feed.summary: Vector and Microchip have expanded their collaboration to offer pre-integrated hardware and software solutions for embedded control units, aiming to simplify development, reduce risk, and accelerate time-to-market. A key outcome is the immediate availability of MICROSAR IO support for Microchip’s dsPIC33A digital signal controllers. This partnership focuses on streamlining development cycles and supporting cost-sensitive sensor and actuator units in Software-Defined Vehicle architectures.

feed.title: 777X
feed.summary: The Boeing 777X family, including the 777-9 and the 777-8 Freighter, is designed for exceptional efficiency and environmental performance. The 777-9 is projected to achieve 20% lower fuel use and emissions, a 40% smaller noise footprint, and 10% lower operating costs compared to competitors. These aircraft offer new aerodynamic breakthroughs, advanced engines, and a spacious cabin, providing the flight experience of the future with seamless integration into Boeing's existing widebody families.

feed.title: Air Separation Plant Market Size, Share | CAGR of 4.7%
feed.summary: The global Air Separation Plant Market is projected to grow from USD 6.4 billion in 2025 to USD 10.2 billion by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 4.7%. This growth is primarily driven by strong demand from the iron & steel, chemical, and healthcare sectors, with Asia Pacific leading the market. Cryogenic air separation technology dominates due to its high efficiency and output volumes, particularly in large industrial applications.

feed.title: Teledyne Marine and M Subs sign MOU to accelerate maritime solutions
feed.summary: Teledyne Marine and M Subs have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to form a strategic partnership focused on accelerating maritime solutions for the UK Royal Navy and international naval markets. This collaboration aims to integrate their respective expertise in unmanned underwater vehicles, autonomy, and marine instrumentation to deliver next-generation solutions for modern maritime operations. Initial efforts include integrating Teledyne SeaBat sonars and deploying Slocum gliders and Osprey AUVs from M Subs' platforms.

feed.title: Nordic Stocks in North America - Autoliv Drops 0.4 Percent
feed.summary: Autoliv (ALV) shares decreased by 0.4% in North American trading. The report details the performance of various Nordic stocks traded in North America, including companies from Sweden, Canada, Denmark, Norway, and Finland, showing their respective changes in share price and volume. The OMX Stockholm 30 Index was also down, reflecting a broader negative trend in the market.

feed.title: Is TE Connectivity Stock Outperforming the S&P 500?
feed.summary: TE Connectivity plc (TEL) has underperformed the S&P 500 over the past three months but has outperformed over the longer term, surging 39.7% over the past 52 weeks compared to SPX's 16.9% returns. The company reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 earnings with strong growth in net sales and adjusted EPS. Despite recent underperformance against a peer like Corning and a slight cooling of momentum, Wall Street maintains a "Strong Buy" rating with significant upside potential.

feed.title: A Look At Atmus Filtration Technologies (ATMU) Valuation As Oil Price Shock Raises Inflation Concerns
feed.summary: Atmus Filtration Technologies (ATMU) experienced a share price decline following a jump in crude oil prices, sparking inflation fears. Despite some recent price momentum, the company's valuation narrative suggests it is 13.6% undervalued with a fair value of $71.60, primarily due to the Koch Filter acquisition. However, its P/E ratio of 24.3x is higher than peers, indicating a potentially tougher valuation picture and less margin for error.

feed.title: ITT Inc. (NYSE:ITT) Given Average Recommendation of "Buy" by Analysts
feed.summary: ITT Inc. (NYSE:ITT) has received a consensus "Buy" rating from nine research firms, with an average 12-month price target of $216.11, and some analysts raising targets to $230-$245. The company recently surpassed Q4 estimates with EPS of $1.85 and revenue of $1.05 billion, and CEO Luca Savi sold a significant portion of his shares. Institutional investors, including Vanguard and Norges Bank, hold 91.59% of the stock.

feed.title: Veeco Instruments Inc. (VECO) Announces Placement of Orders for Multiple Lumina Metal Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition Systems and Spector Ion Beam Sputtering Optical Coating Systems
feed.summary: Veeco Instruments Inc. (NASDAQ: VECO) announced significant orders for its Lumina Metal Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition systems and Spector Ion Beam Sputtering Optical Coating systems from a global leader in optical communications. These systems will be used for fabricating indium phosphide lasers for advanced optical communication solutions in the datacom industry. The company highlights its critical role in InP laser manufacturing and anticipates continued rapid growth in the transceiver market.

feed.title: Stevanato Group Posts Record High‑Value Revenue and Strong 2025 Results, Sets Upbeat 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Stevanato Group (STVN) reported strong financial results for 2025, with record high-value revenue and significant growth in earnings per share. The company's focus on high-value solutions, particularly in biologics and GLP-1 products, drove improved margins and offset declines in other segments. Management provided an upbeat outlook for 2026, forecasting continued revenue growth and profitability gains as new facilities become more efficient.

feed.title: Oceaneering's Momentum: New Electric ROV for 30-Day Subsea Missions - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Oceaneering International has introduced "Momentum," a new electric propulsion work-class remotely operated vehicle (ROV) designed for offshore tasks including drilling, inspection, maintenance, and construction. The ROV is built for high reliability and efficiency, capable of continuous subsea missions for up to thirty days, and is compatible with the company's existing Millennium Plus infrastructure. This new offering aims to enhance operational dependability and handle complex subsea projects.

feed.title: Dr Reddy's Lab gets EIR from USFDA for Srikakulam facility
feed.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories has received an Establishment Inspection Report (EIR) from the USFDA for its Srikakulam formulations manufacturing facility. The inspection outcome was classified as Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI), officially closing the inspection. This inspection included both a Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) review and a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI).

feed.title: Everspin Advances High-Reliability xSPI MRAM Portfolio With 256Mb Density and Production Qualification Milestones
feed.summary: Everspin Technologies has announced significant progress in its high-reliability PERSYST xSPI STT-MRAM portfolio, completing production qualification for its 64Mb MRAM and expanding to a new 256Mb density. The 128Mb device is expected to complete qualification in May 2026, followed by the 256Mb in July 2026, providing scalable memory solutions for demanding applications across various industries. This expansion addresses the need for validated memory with predictable performance in long-lifecycle systems.

feed.title: Why US Bauholz Prices Matter Now: Weyerhaeuser’s Quiet Power Play
feed.summary: The article discusses the resurgence of US lumber (Bauholz) prices and the pivotal role of Weyerhaeuser Co. in this market shift. It highlights how increasing US construction activity and stabilizing mortgage rates are influencing lumber demand and supply, affecting material costs for builders, remodelers, and investors. The piece advises strategic purchasing, monitoring big producers like Weyerhaeuser, and adapting to a structurally tighter, more predictable lumber market compared to post-pandemic volatility.

feed.title: Magnachip Q4 2025 Results: Revenue $40.6M, Provides Q1 2026 Guidance - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Magnachip Semiconductor Corp. reported a Q4 2025 loss of $8.1 million, or 22 cents per share, with adjusted losses at 8 cents per share, on revenues of $40.6 million. For the full year 2025, the company recorded a loss of $29.7 million and revenues of $178.9 million. Magnachip anticipates Q1 2026 revenues to be between $44 million and $48 million.

feed.title: 1 Russell 2000 Stock with Impressive Fundamentals and 2 We Question
feed.summary: This article analyzes three Russell 2000 stocks, highlighting one with strong fundamentals and two that raise concerns. Primoris (PRIM) is recommended as a potential buy due to robust backlog growth, impressive earnings, and growing free cash flow. In contrast, The Marzetti Company (MZTI) and Plug Power (PLUG) are flagged as stocks to avoid, citing unexciting sales trends and commoditized products for Marzetti, and sales declines and cash burn for Plug Power.

feed.title: Everspin Advances High-Reliability xSPI MRAM Portfolio With 256Mb Density and Production Qualification Milestones
feed.summary: Everspin Technologies announced significant progress in its high-reliability PERSYST xSPI STT-MRAM portfolio, including the full production qualification of its 64Mb MRAM and the introduction of a new 256Mb density. The 128Mb and 256Mb xSPI STT-MRAM products are slated for production qualification in May and July 2026, respectively, expanding options for demanding applications in aerospace, defense, and industrial sectors. These advancements provide scalable, validated memory solutions for long-lifecycle systems requiring predictable performance.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Stock Review: Q3 FY2026 Results & Market Position - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) is a large-cap semiconductor company with a market capitalization of $38.6 billion. The stock has outperformed the sector ETF over the past three months, despite a slight decline after its Q3 2026 earnings release where it met revenue estimates and beat EPS. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" consensus rating for MCHP.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport approved for dual listing on Nasdaq Texas, Nasdaq Global exchanges
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. has received approval for a dual listing of its common stock on the new Nasdaq Texas LLC exchange, effective Friday, under the symbol JBHT. This move is aimed at broadening capital access, although it will not affect investors' ability to trade company stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. The company's shares experienced a 3.6% drop on Thursday, closing at $226.36.

feed.title: Toyota, Hyundai, Chery, Stellantis face big disruptions from Iran war, Bernstein analysis shows
feed.summary: A Bernstein analysis suggests that automakers like Toyota, Hyundai, Chery, and Stellantis are exposed to significant disruptions from a conflict involving Iran. The article highlights potential impacts on oil supplies and regional automotive shipments following reported military actions in Tehran. The analysis comes after explosions in Tehran, indicating heightened tensions and a potential threat to the global automotive supply chain.

feed.title: VinFast Auto : Partners with 6 E-scooter Dealers in Indonesia, Accelerating Nationwide Market Expansion
feed.summary: VinFast has signed Memoranda of Understanding with six electric scooter dealers in Indonesia to expand its distribution network and accelerate transport electrification in the region. This move prepares for the Q2/2026 launch of its electric scooters, including models like the VinFast Flazz and Evo, which will be supported by a comprehensive ecosystem of sales, after-sales services, and battery swapping stations. The company aims to establish a strong presence in key Indonesian markets, reflecting its broader strategy to expand e-scooter operations across five international markets in 2026.

feed.title: Mobix Labs: Receives New Gulfstream Aircraft Component Orders as Production Continues
feed.summary: Mobix Labs, Inc. (Nasdaq: MOBX) announced new purchase orders for components supporting Gulfstream aircraft, clarifying that these orders are tied to ongoing aircraft production. The company's high-reliability components are already integrated into Gulfstream systems, supporting critical onboard electronics in complex aircraft environments. These follow-on orders are routine for suppliers whose designs are integrated into platforms with continuous manufacturing activity.

feed.title: Methanex (MEOH) details 2025 global forced and child labour safeguards
feed.summary: Methanex Corporation (MEOH) has released its 2025 Fighting Against Forced and Child Labour Report, outlining comprehensive safeguards and due diligence processes. The report details policies prohibiting forced and child labor across its global operations and supply chain, including mandatory annual training and enhanced contractor assessments. Methanex reported no identified instances of forced or child labor in 2025, with governance oversight from its Corporate Governance Committee.

feed.title: MISTRAS Group Issues Clarification Regarding Diluted EPS
feed.summary: MISTRAS Group, Inc. issued a clarification regarding its Q4 2025 Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items (non-GAAP), stating the correct figure was $0.25 per share, not the inadvertently mentioned $0.20 per share during their earnings call. This clarification ensures accuracy with previously reported financial results and noted that the correct non-GAAP EPS of $0.25 exceeded analyst estimates and represented significant year-over-year growth.

feed.title: Limbach to Participate in CJS Annual New Ideas for the New Year Conference
feed.summary: Limbach Holdings, Inc. announced that its President and CEO, Michael McCann, and EVP and CFO, Jayme Brooks, will participate in the CJS Securities 26th Annual New Ideas for the New Year Conference on January 14, 2026. They will host virtual one-on-one and small group meetings with investors, and a presentation will be webcast. A replay of the presentation will be available on Limbach's Investor Relations Events Calendar.

feed.title: Loma Negra Compania Industrial Argentina Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Loma Negra reported weakened Q4 and full-year 2025 profitability with a 1.7% year-over-year revenue decline and a 33.4% decline in Adjusted EBITDA, primarily due to softer pricing, higher costs, and a challenging comparison base. Despite this, the company strengthened its balance sheet, reducing net debt to $183 million, and completed an oversubscribed $60 million bond issuance. Management anticipates a demand recovery in 2026 with high-single-digit volume growth, driven by infrastructure and energy projects, alongside continued improvements in energy management and notable ESG progress.

feed.title: A Look At Leggett & Platt (LEG) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Leggett & Platt (LEG) has experienced mixed share price performance, with a recent monthly decline of 7.9% but a 32% increase over the past year. The stock is currently trading at $11.23, which is below the analyst target of $12.50, suggesting it may be undervalued by about 22%. The valuation narrative is influenced by modest revenue growth, improved margins, and supply chain flexibility, although risks remain regarding sustained bedding demand and pricing pressure in other segments.

feed.title: OPTICAL CABLE CORPORATION SCHEDULES CONFERENCE CALL TO DISCUSS FIRST QUARTER OF FISCAL YEAR 2026 RESULTS
feed.summary: Optical Cable Corporation (OCC) announced it will release its first quarter of fiscal year 2026 results on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, for the period ending January 31, 2026. The company will also host a conference call on the same day at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time. Interested parties can participate via phone or webcast, and submit questions in advance.

feed.title: First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) CAO Nathan Theurer Sells 92 Shares
feed.summary: First Solar's CAO Nathan Theurer sold 92 shares for approximately $18,119, contributing to negative sentiment following concentrated insider selling. This comes after the company missed Q4 EPS estimates and issued weak 2026 sales guidance, causing shares to drop by 2.8%. Despite these short-term challenges, First Solar shows underlying strength with increased revenue, high net margins, low debt, and plans for U.S. manufacturing expansion.

feed.title: Enovix Corp. (ENVX) Reports $11.3M Q4 Revenue and $31.8M Full-Year 2025 Revenue
feed.summary: Enovix Corporation reported a strong financial performance for 2025, with annual revenue reaching $31.8 million, a 38% increase from 2024, and record Q4 revenue of $11.3 million due to battery shipments for defense and industrial applications. The company is actively working to optimize its technology for high-volume smartphone production and sees significant early commercial opportunities in smart eyewear. For Q1 2026, Enovix projects revenues between $6.5 million and $7.5 million, with a non-GAAP net loss per share of $0.14 to $0.18.

feed.title: Insider Sell: W Will Sells 64,273 Shares of CF Industries Holdin
feed.summary: A Director at CF Industries Holdings Inc (CF), W Will, sold 64,273 shares of the company on March 3, 2026. This transaction increases Will's total sales over the past year to 413,579 shares, with no corresponding purchases. Following the sale, CF Industries Holdings Inc is considered modestly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.1, trading at $109.06 per share.

feed.title: Fastest rise in global export orders in over four years
feed.summary: Global export orders experienced their fastest rise in over four years in February, primarily driven by growth in emerging markets and a strong rebound in manufacturing and services sectors. The S&P Global PMI New Export Orders rose to 51.2, marking the first expansion in nearly a year, though this data precedes recent geopolitical developments like the new conflict in the Middle East and US tariff decisions. Mainland China notably led the growth in goods export orders, while American economies showed the weakest performance.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Natarajan Ramanan Sells Shares of Energy Recovery Inc (ERII)
feed.summary: Energy Recovery Inc's Chief Technology Officer, Natarajan Ramanan, sold 272 shares of the company on March 5, 2026, reducing his holdings to 52,565 shares. The stock is currently trading at $10.57, which is significantly undervalued compared to its GF Value of $16.78. Over the past year, Insider transaction history for Energy Recovery Inc shows more selling activity with 2 insider buys and 16 insider sells.

feed.title: Littelfuse Appoints Holly B. Paeper to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Littelfuse, Inc. (NASDAQ: LFUS) has announced the appointment of Holly B. Paeper to its board of directors, effective March 4, 2026. Ms. Paeper, who is currently the President, Commercial HVAC Americas, for Trane Technologies, brings extensive leadership experience in end markets related to Littelfuse's mission. She will also serve on the Technology Committee, leveraging her expertise in driving strategic growth to benefit the company.

feed.title: American Century Companies Inc. Decreases Stake in Littelfuse, Inc.
feed.summary: American Century Companies Inc. reduced its stake in Littelfuse, Inc. by 29.2% in the third quarter of 2026, selling 101,350 shares and now owning 245,177 shares valued at $63.5 million. This move by the global investment management firm could indicate a shift in investor sentiment, even though Littelfuse continues to have high institutional ownership. Littelfuse specializes in circuit protection, power control, and sensing technologies.

feed.title: Investment Analysts Raise Price Targets for CF Industries
feed.summary: Investment analysts have raised price targets for CF Industries (NYSE: CF), with Barclays PLC increasing its target to $120 per share. This positive sentiment is reinforced by the company's strong financial performance, a recently announced quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, and its position as a major producer of agricultural and industrial chemicals. However, a recent insider stock sale by EVP Susan L. Menzel bears watching, as she sold 3,215 shares.

feed.title: Caterpillar Built A Real Pickup Truck After AI Faked One First
feed.summary: Inspired by AI-generated images, Caterpillar has developed a one-off pickup truck based on a Ford Super Duty. Unveiled at an industry event, the "Cat Truck" features custom yellow-and-black styling, off-road accessories, and Caterpillar's VisionLink infotainment system. While the company has no plans for mass production, this custom build showcases Caterpillar's design vision in the automotive space.

feed.title: Applied Materials Stock Down 3.1% - Time to Sell?
feed.summary: Shares of Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) fell 3.1% on Thursday, prompting investor questions despite the stock's strong 40% gain over the past year. As a bellwether for the semiconductor industry, its performance reflects broader tech trends. Analysts maintain "Buy" ratings, suggesting the dip is likely a short-term fluctuation for the industry leader.

feed.title: AbbVie to invest $380 million in North Chicago campus to make next generation medicines
feed.summary: AbbVie plans to invest $380 million in two new manufacturing facilities at its North Chicago campus to produce next-generation neuroscience and obesity medications, integrating advanced manufacturing and AI. This investment is part of AbbVie's $100 billion commitment to U.S. R&D and capital investments, adding to a previous $195 million expansion announced last August. The project is expected to create 300 new jobs and is hailed by local officials as a significant win for the region's biomanufacturing ecosystem.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Eric Chewning Sells Shares of Huntington Ingalls I
feed.summary: Eric Chewning, Executive Vice President at Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (HII), sold 1,700 shares of the company on March 4, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 1,949.488 shares. The stock is currently trading at $433.44 and is considered significantly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.58.

feed.title: Enovix Corp. (ENVX) Reports $11.3M Q4 Revenue and $31.8M Full-Year 2025 Revenue
feed.summary: Enovix Corporation announced its 2025 financial results, with annual revenue reaching $31.8 million, showing a 38% increase from 2024. The company's Q4 revenue also hit a record $11.3 million, largely due to battery shipments for defense and industrial applications. Enovix is focusing on completing smartphone qualification and optimizing production at its Fab2 facility, while also seeing significant early commercial opportunity in smart eyewear.

feed.title: Stellantis Pro One, Stellantis’ Commercial Vehicles Business Unit, launches in Europe a commercial campaign offering BEVs at diesel price
feed.summary: Stellantis Pro One is launching a commercial campaign in Europe offering battery-electric versions of its compact and mid-size vans at the same price as their diesel equivalents. This initiative aims to remove the purchase price barrier for EV adoption among professional customers, ensuring access to sustainable mobility without compromising operational capabilities. The offer is valid until the end of June 2026 and covers a wide range of popular van models from Citroën, Fiat Professional, Opel, and Peugeot.

feed.title: FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN plummeted following exacerbated geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which drove oil prices higher and reignited inflation concerns. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped significantly due to fear of disrupted global energy markets and potential delays in Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. While the market saw a broad-based sell-off across multiple sectors, these five companies experienced notable declines in share price.

feed.title: U.S. NRC Dockets Terra Innovatum’s SOLO™ Micro-Modular Reactor Topical Reports, Initiating the Formal Review and Approval Process
feed.summary: Terra Innovatum Global N.V. announced that the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has formally docketed several key topical reports for its SOLO™ Micro-Modular Reactor, marking a significant regulatory milestone. This action initiates the NRC's detailed technical review, moving the company closer to obtaining approval for construction and operation. The company aims for commercial deployment of its carbon-free power solution by 2028, targeting energy-intensive infrastructure and other critical applications.

feed.title: Is RBC Bearings (RBC) Pricing In Too Much Optimism After Strong Multi Year Share Gains
feed.summary: RBC Bearings (RBC) has seen significant share gains over the past five years, but a Simply Wall St analysis suggests the stock might be overvalued. Based on Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio evaluations, RBC's current share price of $566.06 is considerably higher than its estimated intrinsic value of $372.94 and its P/E ratio is well above industry averages, indicating potential over-optimism in its current market pricing.

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical's Long-Term Growth Intact, but Margin Expansion Less Uncertain
feed.summary: Morningstar analyst Jay Lee provides an update on West Pharmaceutical Services, reiterating that the company's long-term growth prospects remain intact. However, the report also suggests that margin expansion for West Pharmaceutical is becoming less certain. The article highlights the company's position as a global market leader in primary packaging and delivery components for injectable therapeutics and notes recent strong earnings due to solid margin improvement.

feed.title: Wabtec (WAB): Should You Buy, Sell, or Hold After Q4 Results?
feed.summary: Wabtec (WAB) has shown impressive financial performance, delivering a 249% return since March 2021 and outperforming the S&P 500. feed.title: How Boeing Fighters Stack Up Against Lockheed Martin In 2026
feed.summary: This article compares the fighter aircraft production and philosophies of Boeing and Lockheed Martin in 2026, highlighting their differing approaches to design, manufacturing, and tactical missions. Lockheed Martin dominates with its F-35 program, focusing on stealth and multinational production, while Boeing emphasizes conventional fighter performance and raw power with its F-15EX and F-18 Super Hornet, and is investing in next-generation stealth technology. The piece details how these aircraft are designed to work together, forming a "high-low" fleet strategy for the US Air Force and Navy.

The collaboration signifies a crucial shift in the automotive industry towards centralized, software-driven vehicle control, creating a formidable competitor to other SDV initiatives.

feed.title: Layoffs at Campbell's Soup factory near Oklahoma border top 200

In Thailand, it will be called the e:N2 and is set to launch at the Bangkok International Motor Show with a price of around 1.4 million baht, while in Japan, it will revive the Insight nameplate with pre-orders beginning in March. Both versions will be manufactured by Dongfeng-Honda in China and will offer similar specifications, including a 150 kW motor and a 68.8 kWh battery.

These tensions led to a surge in oil prices, reigniting inflation concerns and impacting the broader market. The instability complicates monetary policy for the Federal Reserve and raises fears of increased fuel costs and reduced consumer spending.

feed.title: O-I Glass Announces Leadership Change in Sales and Marketing

feed.title: Littelfuse Expands Board, Appoints Independent Technology Director

Paeper as an independent director and member of its Technology Committee, effective March 4, 2026. Paeper's expertise in thermal management, building technologies, and data center solutions is expected to bolster Littelfuse's strategic positioning, especially given existing commercial ties with Trane, where she leads a business unit. This appointment aims to enhance the board's technological oversight and strategic perspective in key end markets.

feed.title: Nvidia Suspends H200 AI Chip Production for China Market
feed.summary: Nvidia has halted production of its H200 AI chips intended for the Chinese market, reallocating manufacturing capacity at TSMC to focus on its next-generation Vera Rubin architecture. This strategic shift is a response to tightening US-China semiconductor controls, prolonged export permit delays, and China's push for domestic chip procurement. The move ensures Nvidia prioritizes hardware for Western clients and optimizes production for less restrictive markets, while existing H200 inventory holds for potential limited demand.

feed.title: Oil & Gas Commission approves Tetra Technologies additional compensation rate for lithium
feed.summary: The Arkansas Department of Energy and Environment, Oil and Gas Commission, has approved an additional lithium compensation rate of 2.5% for TETRA Technologies, Inc. This approval, consistent with previous decisions for other companies, applies to lithium production from TETRA's Evergreen Brine Production Unit and Evergreen Brine Expansion Unit in south Arkansas. The decision aims to further position Arkansas as a leader in lithium production, providing clear compensation rates and encouraging extraction methods with minimal environmental impact.

feed.title: As Frank Yeary retires from Intel, the company picks an engineer to chair its board — Intel Foundry governance issues to be resolved, and looking back at the Yeary years
feed.summary: Frank D. Yeary is retiring as Intel's independent board chair, to be succeeded by Dr. Craig H. Barratt, an engineer with extensive semiconductor and networking experience. Yeary's tenure coincided with significant financial losses and reports of disagreement with CEO Lip-Bu Tan over the future of Intel Foundry. Barratt's appointment signals a renewed focus on scaling U.S. R&D and manufacturing, providing stability for Intel Foundry as it strives for profitability and acceptance of its 18A process node.

feed.title: F-150 Lightning Outsold Tesla's Cybertruck, But Ford CEO Says He 'Would've Done It Differently'
feed.summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley expressed regrets about the company's electric vehicle strategy, particularly with the F-150 Lightning, despite it outselling Tesla's Cybertruck in 2025. Farley acknowledged that Ford lost money on each electric pickup sold and plans to focus on profitability with hybrids and gas-powered vehicles, while later introducing a more affordable EV platform. He also highlighted China's rapid advancements in the automotive sector and Ford's new focus on vehicles as "entertainment space."

feed.title: Aptiv Board of Directors Approves Spin-Off of Versigent
feed.summary: Aptiv's Board of Directors has approved the spin-off of its Electrical Distribution Systems business into a new publicly traded company, Versigent. The spin-off will be effective April 1, 2026, with Aptiv shareholders receiving one ordinary share of Versigent for every three Aptiv shares held as of the March 17, 2026 record date. Versigent shares are expected to trade on the NYSE under the ticker symbol "VGNT".

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of GrafTech International Ltd. - EAF
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of GrafTech International Ltd. ($EAF) regarding alleged securities fraud or unlawful business practices. This investigation follows a significant drop in GrafTech's stock price after the company reported a challenging fourth quarter and full-year 2025, with an adjusted loss per share exceeding estimates and intensifying competitive pricing pressures for graphite electrodes. The firm is inviting affected investors to contact them for information about joining a potential class action.

feed.title: Quanex Building Products Corporation (NX) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings: Modest Revenue Growth but EPS Miss
feed.summary: Quanex Building Products Corporation (NX) reported modest revenue growth of 2.3% to $409.1 million in Q1 fiscal 2026, but missed EPS estimates with -$0.09. The company saw stronger gross margins but a sharp contraction in operating profitability and net income, despite an improvement in cash on hand. Insider and hedge fund activity showed mixed sentiment, with some major institutional investors increasing while others significantly decreasing their positions.

feed.title: Methode: Fiscal Q3 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Methode Electronics Inc. reported a loss of $15.9 million in its fiscal third quarter, translating to 45 cents per share, or an adjusted loss of 37 cents per share. The electrical components manufacturer posted revenue of $233.7 million for the period and projects full-year revenue between $950 million and $1 billion.

feed.title: RTX Announces Board Resignation and Governance Adjustment

Following his departure, the Board's size was reduced from eleven to ten members, representing a minor governance adjustment. Analysts currently rate RTX stock as a Buy with a $240.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it as Outperform, citing improving financial performance despite valuation concerns and near-term headwinds.

feed.title: Fertilizer Stocks To Watch Today - March 5th

These companies are chosen for their high dollar trading volume and provide exposure to global crop demand and commodity fertilizer prices. Fertilizer stocks are inherently cyclical, influenced by factors such as weather, crop cycles, input costs like natural gas and potash, and governmental trade policies.

feed.title: Methode Electronics, Inc. Sells dataMate Copper Transceiver Business to Bel Fuse Inc. to Focus on Power Solutions

The dataMate business, known for its innovation in data-over-copper solutions, is expected to benefit from Bel Fuse's resources.

feed.title: Yard Management: Grounds for Improvement

Facing challenges with increased trailer volume, manual processes, and safety concerns, the YMS provided real-time visibility, automated driver coordination, and cut trailer movement times drastically. The system's user-friendly interface and Kaleris's responsive support were key factors in Westrock's success and continued expansion.

feed.title: Diamond-infused cooling plate from Coherent targets hotter AI chips
feed.summary: Coherent (NYSE: COHR) has launched Thermadite 800 Liquid Cold Plates (LCPs) designed for cooling high-power AI accelerators. This new material, which integrates diamond into a SiC matrix, offers double the thermal conductivity of copper and can reduce chip temperatures by over 15°C. It also provides a 60% lower density than copper, enabling lighter and more efficient cooling solutions for mass-sensitive, high-heat-flux systems.

feed.title: General Dynamics Mission Systems Continues Support of Trident II Strategic Weapon System with New Contract
feed.summary: General Dynamics Mission Systems secured a follow-on contract with an initial order value of $255 million to continue its role as prime integrator for the Trident II Fire Control System (FCS). The agreement ensures full life cycle support for Ohio-class ballistic missile submarine FCSs and development for new Columbia-class SSBN FCSs through 2032, with options potentially increasing the total value to $740 million. This contract also includes continuous development for the Trident II D5 Life Extension 2 (D5LE2) FCS and supports the Navy’s shipbuilding efforts.

feed.title: Eaton Corp. Faces Weak Start with Gap Down Amid Market Concerns
feed.summary: Eaton Corp. (Electronics & Appliances sector) experienced a significant gap down, opening with a 3.87% loss and a 5.79% decrease over the past day, underperforming the S&P 500. Despite strong financial metrics like a USD 145 billion market cap, a P/E ratio of 36.00, and a 114.46% dividend yield, technical indicators show a mixed outlook with some bearish signals for the short term. The article discusses its recent performance and financial health amid market concerns.

feed.title: Daqo New Energy Corp. reports FY2025 financial results
feed.summary: Daqo New Energy Corp. announced its FY2025 financial results, showing a decline in revenue to $665.4 million due to lower polysilicon prices and reduced sales volumes. Despite a gross loss, the company improved its EBITDA to $1.7 million from a negative $337.4 million in FY2024. The net loss attributable to shareholders also decreased, and the company projects increased polysilicon production for FY2026.

feed.title: Campbell Soup (Texas) WARN Act Investigation
feed.summary: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating Campbell Soup Supply Company LLC regarding a potential mass layoff of 205 employees in Paris, Texas, on March 3, 2026. The firm believes Campbell Soup may have violated the federal WARN Act by potentially failing to provide the required 60-day notice to employees before the layoff. Employees impacted by this layoff may be entitled to 60 days of severance pay and benefits, and the law firm is seeking to speak with affected individuals.

feed.title: Is Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (APD) A Good Stock To Buy Now?

APD features a strong dividend growth streak and an attractive risk-reward profile, with major capital expenditures winding down, forecast to boost free cash flow.

feed.title: Mining giant Mosaic seeks federal approval to expand its waste pile near Tampa Bay
feed.summary: Mosaic, a major fertilizer company, is seeking federal approval to expand its phosphogypsum waste pile near Tampa Bay by nearly 180 acres. This expansion, which involves filling about 35 acres of wetlands, would extend the life of its Hillsborough County plant by 16 years. Environmental groups have raised concerns about the project's proximity to Tampa Bay and its potential impact, especially given its fast-tracking under a federal program, while Mosaic also proposes environmental protections for bird islands.

feed.title: Layoffs at Campbell's Soup factory near Oklahoma border top 200

The Radial Trail A/T offers features like aggressive traction, optimized fuel efficiency, and heat control, while the Radial Trail HD's updated N-rating signifies exceptional performance and durability. feed.title: Paccar Profits Up Despite Slower Truck Sales

feed.title: GE Aerospace Engine Deals Broaden Defense Role While Valuation Stays Stretched

While these projects demonstrate the company's innovation, its shares are currently trading about 26.4% above Simply Wall St's estimated fair value, indicating a stretched valuation.

feed.title: Ford recalls more than 600K vehicles over faulty windshield wipers
feed.summary: Ford is recalling over 600,000 vehicles, including certain 2020-2022 Explorer, Escape, Lincoln Aviator, and Lincoln Corsair models, due to faulty windshield wiper motors that can fail and reduce visibility. The issue stems from a poor connection during assembly. Although Ford initially closed an investigation in 2021, it was reopened in late 2025 after a surge in warranty claims, with repairs offered free of charge to affected owners.

feed.title: Ford recalls more than 600K vehicles over faulty windshield wipers
feed.summary: Ford is recalling over 600,000 vehicles, including certain 2020-2022 Explorer, Escape, Lincoln Aviator, and Lincoln Corsair models, due to faulty windshield wiper motors. The defect, stemming from a poor connection during assembly, can cause wipers to fail, reducing driver visibility and increasing accident risk. Ford reopened its investigation in November 2025 after an increase in claims, though no related incidents or injuries have been reported.
