feed.summary: GLJ Research has initiated coverage of nVent Electric (NVT) with a "Buy" rating and a price target of $151.00 USD. This new rating follows several other positive analyst reports from February 2026, which maintained or raised price targets, reflecting consistent confidence in the company's market position and growth prospects. Analysts have an average target price of $125.51 for NVT, implying a 13.78% upside from its current price.

feed.title: Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO) Secures Authorization for Core Product Placement Across 1,000 U.S. Retail Locations
feed.summary: Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO) has secured authorization from a national rural lifestyle retail partner for the placement of one of its core products across 1,000 U.S. retail locations. This initial rollout includes approximately 2,400 units, supported by both physical stores and an online platform, creating an omnichannel sales approach. The agreement also provides a scalable pathway for potential expansion to the retailer's roughly 2,200 U.S. locations based on performance.

feed.title: Massimo Group secures placement in 1,000 retail locations By Investing.com
feed.summary: Massimo Group (NASDAQ:MAMO) has announced that one of its products will be placed in 1,000 U.S. retail locations of a national rural lifestyle retailer, representing a significant portion of the retailer's 2,200 U.S. stores. This expansion follows Massimo reporting $70.9 million in revenue over the past year, and comes amid other developments like a proposed acquisition of AI firm FST Development Company Limited and the launch of the Sentinel 770 HVAC UTV. InvestingPro suggests the stock is currently undervalued.

feed.title: UniFirst (UNF) Shares Surge on Reported Cintas Talks
feed.summary: UniFirst (UNF) stock surged by 13% after reports emerged of potential discussions with Cintas, driving its share price to $261.38. Despite this surge, UniFirst, a provider of uniform and facility services, faces challenges like slowing revenue growth and declining operating margins, as highlighted by a mixed financial analysis. While the company exhibits strong financial health with robust liquidity, its valuation metrics suggest the stock might be overvalued, and recent insider selling warrants attention from investors.

feed.title: Donaldson Co. Earns CARB Verification
feed.summary: Donaldson Co. Inc. has received verification from the California Air Resources Board (CARB) for its diesel particulate matter low-temperature filter (LTF) muffler. This product, designed for 1994-2006 non-EGR diesel engines using ultra-low sulfur diesel, achieves at least an 85% reduction in particulate matter, qualifying it as an ARB Level 3 device. The LTF muffler, which can be used with or without the Spiracle crankcase filter system, is now available nationwide due to EPA CARB Verification Reciprocity.

feed.title: B. Riley Financial Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Hudson Technologies (NASDAQ:HDSN) Stock Price
feed.summary: B. Riley Financial has lowered its price target for Hudson Technologies (NASDAQ:HDSN) shares from $10.50 to $9.50, while still maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential 46.38% upside. This adjustment comes amidst mixed analyst ratings, with the stock currently having an average "Hold" rating and an average price target of $9.50. Hudson Technologies recently missed EPS estimates but exceeded revenue forecasts, and its shares experienced a decline during Thursday's trading.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean wants to build something huge in Panama to change how ships get repaired
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has proposed building a massive floating dry dock on Panama's Pacific coast. This facility, capable of servicing large cruise and container ships, aims to provide an alternative to busy Asian and North American dry docks, create 500-800 jobs, and enhance Panama's maritime hub status. The project is planned for development from 2026 through 2031, with a focus on environmental sustainability.

feed.title: Jabil sent to move manufacturing out of downtown Clinton; NyproMold to stay
feed.summary: Jabil, a global manufacturing services provider, plans to cease its manufacturing operations in downtown Clinton by the end of November 2026, consolidating production to its Devens facility. This decision follows a comprehensive review of its manufacturing footprint. Despite the move, two subsidiaries, NyproMold and NP Medical, will continue to operate at the Clinton complex without disruption.

feed.title: Jabil set to move manufacturing out of downtown Clinton; NyproMold to stay
feed.summary: Jabil, a global manufacturing services provider that acquired Nypro Inc. in 2013, plans to cease its manufacturing operations in downtown Clinton by the end of November 2026, consolidating production at its Devens facility. While Jabil's manufacturing unit will leave, its subsidiaries NyproMold and NP Medical will continue to operate at the Clinton complex. The company has pledged support for affected employees through severance benefits, outplacement assistance, and opportunities to transfer to other Jabil locations.

feed.title: Fastenal Reports a More than 13% Jump in February Sales
feed.summary: Fastenal announced a 13.3% increase in February sales compared to the previous year, reaching $710.6 million. The growth was observed across all geographies, end markets, and customer segments, with significant jumps in heavy manufacturing, non-residential construction, OEM, and MRO categories. This follows a double-digit year-over-year increase in January and an 11.1% increase in its latest fiscal quarter.

feed.title: Deere & Co Stock (DE) Moved Down by 3.95% on Mar 5: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Deere & Company's stock dropped by 3.95% on March 5th, primarily due to investor concerns about the agricultural equipment market, despite the company raising its full-year 2026 net income guidance. Although Q1 2026 net income decreased by 25% year-over-year, overall revenue surpassed analyst expectations. Investors are focusing on the underlying weakness and profit pressures in the large agriculture segment, exacerbated by tariffs, production costs, and a "slow demand cycle" expected to bottom in 2026.

feed.title: Reasons Why You Should Retain Aptiv Stock in Your Portfolio
feed.summary: Aptiv PLC (APTV) is positioned for growth due to high demand for its ADAS systems in the automotive industry, along with smart strategies, acquisitions, and strategic investments. The company is expected to see revenue growth and increased earnings in 2026 and 2027. Despite operating in a competitive market, APTV's financial health, evidenced by its current ratio, indicates its ability to meet short-term obligations, making it a stock to consider retaining.

feed.title: John Deere expands self-repair tools for farmers
feed.summary: John Deere has empowered farmers with advanced self-repair capabilities through new digital tools. These tools, including the Equipment Mobile app, the Operations Center, and the Pro Service platform, provide farmers with access to maintenance information, diagnostic codes, and step-by-step repair guides. The initiative aims to help farmers maintain and repair their equipment efficiently, even offline, ensuring minimal downtime.

feed.title: A US company you've never heard of lost more than $16 million because of Trump
feed.summary: Weyco Group Inc., a Wisconsin-based shoe company, lost over $16 million due to tariffs imposed by former President Trump, leading to a 24 percent drop in profits. The company passed some costs to consumers and rerouted production overseas. Weyco Group Inc. has since sued the Trump administration to recoup its losses, especially after the Supreme Court ruled that Trump's use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to justify tariffs was an overreach.

feed.title: AMETEK Inc. stock: Is this quiet US tech giant your next sleeper win?
feed.summary: AMETEK Inc. is positioned as a quiet but significant player in industrial technology, manufacturing high-precision instruments and electromechanical devices used across various sectors like aerospace, EVs, and medical devices. The article suggests it might be an attractive "sleeper win" for investors seeking long-term growth from a US-centric company with consistent earnings, a robust acquisition strategy, and a focus on essential industrial applications rather than consumer hype. While experts note its strong margins and diversified markets, they also caution about potential valuation risks and cyclical exposure.

feed.title: DE Gains 33% in a Year: What's the Right Strategy for Investors Now?
feed.summary: Deere & Company (DE) shares have outperformed the market, gaining 33.1% in a year, leading investors to consider its prospects. Despite near-term challenges from low crop prices and tariff impacts on Q1 earnings, DE's long-term growth remains robust due to innovation, geographic expansion, and acquisitions in advanced technologies. However, the stock currently trades at a premium valuation compared to its industry peers and its own historical median, suggesting new investors might want to wait for a better entry point.

feed.title: Gibraltar Industries (NASDAQ:ROCK) Reaches New 1-Year Low - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Gibraltar Industries (NASDAQ:ROCK) stock dropped to a new 52-week low of $42.16, driven primarily by Sidoti's fiscal year 2026 EPS estimate of $3.78, which is significantly below the consensus of $4.91. Despite a slight beat on last quarter's earnings and revenue, the company's FY2026 guidance missed analyst forecasts, contributing to negative market sentiment. Analyst ratings for ROCK are split between "Strong Buy" and "Sell," resulting in a consensus "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Commercial Vehicle Group Teams With Volvo for Truck Seating
feed.summary: Commercial Vehicle Group Inc. has announced a partnership with Volvo 3P to develop a global modular seating platform for medium and heavy-duty trucks. This collaboration aims to meet increasing global expectations for safety, ergonomics, and durability, with the modular seat systems designed according to Volvo's "Best in Class Engineered Product" (BICEP) criteria. The partnership is expected to launch initially in Europe and then expand to other global production sites.

feed.title: CSW Industrials Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Dynamics
feed.summary: CSW Industrials, Inc. has revised its stock evaluation due to changing market conditions, with its stock currently priced at $298.38. Technical indicators show mixed sentiments, with weekly MACD being mildly bearish, and daily moving averages suggesting a mildly bullish trend. While the company saw a 2.26% decline in the past year, it significantly outperformed the S&P 500 over a three-year period with a 108.18% return.

feed.title: Podcast: Sanofi's head of clinical supply chain operations on evolving the clinical trial supply model
feed.summary: This article features a podcast interview with Arnaud Dourlens, Sanofi's head of clinical supply chain operations. He discusses the challenges facing existing clinical trial supply models, such as increased competition, trial complexity, and pricing pressures. Dourlens highlights how technological advancements and evolving leadership can help drive operational resilience and mitigate these ongoing pressures.

feed.title: Columbus McKinnon (CMCO) reshapes portfolio with Kito buy and U.S. hoist sale
feed.summary: Columbus McKinnon (CMCO) has strategically reshaped its portfolio by completing the sale of its U.S. power chain hoist and chain manufacturing operations for $210 million, with a potential $25 million earnout. Concurrently, the company finalized the $2.7 billion acquisition of Kito Crosby, funded through a combination of new secured debt, senior secured notes, and convertible preferred shares. Pro forma financial statements indicate increased interest and amortization expenses due to the acquisition, leading to projected net losses attributable to common shareholders.

feed.title: Plexus Earns ISO 14001 Certification in Mexico
feed.summary: Plexus Corp.'s Manufacturing Solutions facility in Guadalajara, Mexico, has achieved ISO 14001 certification, recognizing its commitment to environmental management. This certification emphasizes the company's focus on measuring and improving its environmental impact. The facility, which opened in August 2014, also holds ISO 9001 and ISO 13485 standards certifications.

feed.title: Trinity Industries declares 31-cent quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Trinity Industries Inc. (NYSE:TRN) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per share, marking its 248th consecutive quarterly payment and extending a 56-year streak. InvestingPro analysis indicates the company has raised its dividend for 15 consecutive years and is currently undervalued. This announcement follows strong Q4 2025 financial results where the company significantly beat EPS and revenue expectations.

feed.title: MISTRAS Group Issues Clarification Regarding Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items (Non-GAAP) Referenced During Q4 2025 Earnings Call
feed.summary: MISTRAS Group, Inc. has issued a clarification regarding its Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items (non-GAAP) for Q4 2025 and the full year 2025. During their Q4 2025 earnings call, management mistakenly stated the Q4 diluted EPS as $0.20, when the correct figure, as reported in official documents, is $0.25 per share. The company noted that the $0.25 diluted EPS exceeded analyst estimates and that the full-year diluted EPS of $0.88 represents a significant increase from 2024.

feed.title: Teledyne e2v Introduces Perciva™ 5D Camera: Occlusion-free 3D Vision for Industrial, Retail, and Robotic Imaging
feed.summary: Teledyne e2v has launched the Perciva™ 5D camera, an innovative imaging solution providing cost-effective and reliable short-range 3D vision for industrial, retail, and robotic applications. The camera uses unique Angular Sensitive Pixel technology and a Neural Processing Unit to generate 2D and 3D data from a single CMOS sensor without optical occlusion, even in ambient light. It features plug-and-play integration and is suitable for various challenging environments, including robotics and self-checkout systems.

feed.title: MISTRAS Group, Inc. Clarifies Q4 2025 Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items Reporting
feed.summary: MISTRAS Group, Inc. has issued a clarification regarding its Q4 2025 diluted EPS excluding special items, stating the correct figure is $0.25 per share, not the $0.20 mentioned in their earnings call. This corrected amount surpasses analyst expectations of $0.21. The company also reported a full-year diluted EPS of $0.88 for 2025, an increase of 22.2% from 2024, reaffirming its financial health and transparency.

feed.title: Norwegian Luna Officially Delivered as NCL Expands Its Prima Plus Fleet
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line has taken delivery of the Norwegian Luna, the second ship in its expanded Prima Plus Class, from Italian shipbuilder Fincantieri. The 156,000-gross-ton vessel, accommodating 3,565 passengers, will debut in Miami on March 23, 2026, offering Bahamas and Caribbean itineraries. This delivery strengthens the long-standing partnership between NCLH and Fincantieri, with the Luna providing diverse entertainment and dining options for guests.

feed.title: New 5D camera lets robots and self-checkouts see in 3D
feed.summary: Teledyne e2v has launched the Perciva™ 5D camera, an innovative imaging solution designed to provide cost-effective, reliable, and easy-to-integrate high-quality short-range 3D vision. This new camera leverages a unique Angular Sensitive Pixel technology and an on-board Neural Processing Unit to generate both 2D and 3D data from a single CMOS sensor, free from optical occlusion. It's ideal for robotics, retail self-checkout systems, and industrial process monitoring, operating efficiently in various lighting conditions without requiring an external NIR source.

feed.title: Lennox International (NYSE:LII) Given New $560.00 Price Target at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Lennox International (NYSE:LII) to $560.00 from $520.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 5.09% for the construction company's stock. The article also notes other analyst ratings, recent insider stock sales, and institutional trading activity for Lennox International.

feed.title: General Electric Co Stock (GE) Moved Down by 3.35% on Mar 5: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: General Electric (GE) stock fell by 3.35% on March 5th, influenced by broader market concerns such as geopolitical tensions and inflation, as well as company-specific issues like a potential engine seal problem for Boeing's 777X and valuation concerns. Despite these pressures, the company's fundamentals remain strong with positive analyst ratings and price targets. The movement highlights a dynamic trading environment where immediate risks clash with long-term bullish perspectives.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Industrial Goods Companies: Byrna Technologies (BYRN) and Mayville Engineering Company (MEC)
feed.summary: Analysts have provided insights on two industrial goods companies: Byrna Technologies (BYRN) and Mayville Engineering Company (MEC). Craig-Hallum reiterated a Buy rating for Byrna Technologies with a $27.00 price target, while Northland Securities maintained a Hold rating for Mayville Engineering Company with a $20.00 price target. The Street consensus suggests a Strong Buy for Byrna and a Moderate Buy for Mayville Engineering Company.

feed.title: Jeep turns 85, adds limited editions and first electric Trail Rated SUV
feed.summary: Jeep is celebrating its 85th anniversary by unveiling special 85th Anniversary editions for several models, including the Wrangler, Gladiator, Cherokee, Grand Cherokee, Grand Wagoneer, and Compass. These special editions feature unique styling cues such as Agave Blue badging and Mayan Gold stitching. The brand also confirmed the upcoming launch of its first all-electric Trail Rated SUV, the Jeep Recon, later in 2026, highlighting its history of 4x4 innovation.

feed.title: NN (NASDAQ:NNBR) Announces Quarterly Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.31 EPS
feed.summary: NN (NASDAQ:NNBR) reported quarterly earnings results, announcing a loss of $0.29 EPS, missing analysts' consensus estimates by $0.31. The industrial products company posted revenue of $104.72 million, slightly below the $105.36 million estimate. Following the announcement, NN shares fell approximately 4.2% to $1.47.

feed.title: Honda limits tariff impact via logistics, procurement tweaks
feed.summary: Honda managed to reduce the impact of tariffs for the fiscal year ending March 31 from 450 billion yen to 310 billion yen by implementing logistics adjustments and increasing procurement that met USMCA requirements. Despite recording a 267 billion yen operating loss due to shifts in U.S. EV policy and ongoing supply chain risks, the company is fundamentally reviewing its strategies to rebuild competitive strength. A new risk identified is the rare earth supply chain, affected by China's export restrictions, prompting Honda to implement strategies like filing export applications promptly, holding extra inventory, and exploring redesigning components to remove rare earth elements.

feed.title: Babcock & Wilcox Q4 2025 Profit & Strong Stock Performance | Financial Report - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises Inc. returned to profitability in Q4 2025 with a net income of $9.2 million, reversing a loss from the previous year. The company's revenue for the quarter was $161 million, and its stock performance has been strong, increasing by 28% year-to-date and sevenfold over the last twelve months. The article also outlines various market research sections offered by IndexBox.

feed.title: Miller Industries (MLR) Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 5%
feed.summary: Miller Industries (MLR) has announced a 5% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.21 per share. The company, which specializes in vehicle towing and recovery equipment, demonstrates strong financial health with robust revenue growth and profitability, though some valuation ratios are near historical highs. While its financial fundamentals are strong, potential valuation concerns and higher volatility compared to the market suggest investors should carefully consider these factors.

feed.title: Trimble and Hyundai partner on fast-track upgrade from 2D to 3D grade control
feed.summary: Trimble Inc. and Hyundai Construction Equipment have announced a collaboration to allow contractors to upgrade select Hyundai excavators from factory-installed 2D grade control systems to the Trimble Earthworks 3D platform. This initiative, unveiled at CONEXPO 2026, provides a streamlined aftermarket upgrade path, including Trimble's "autos" mode for automated boom and bucket movements, without needing to replace existing machinery. The partnership aims to help civil contractors scale their technology as project complexity increases, offering higher accuracy and productivity amidst skilled labor shortages.

feed.title: Leidos Holdings (LDOS) to Offer its Small Cruise Missile to the U.S. Air Force
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings (NYSE:LDOS) will offer its Small Cruise Missile, officially designated AGM-190A, to the U.S. Air Force, recognizing it as an affordable, adaptable stand-off strike solution. The 200-pound weapon demonstrated a stand-off range of over 400 nautical miles and features modular hardware and open-system software for evolving mission requirements. This development aligns with Leidos's NorthStar 2030 strategy and demonstrates the Air Force's confidence in the company's scalable defense capabilities.

feed.title: IDEX Stock Exhibits Strong Prospects Despite Persisting Headwinds
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation (IEX) is experiencing strong performance in its Fluid & Metering Technologies (FMT) and Health & Science Technologies (HST) segments, driven by demand for water business products and solid momentum in pharma and semiconductor sectors. The company is also expanding through strategic acquisitions, such as Micro-LAM and Mott Corp., to broaden its technological offerings. Despite these positives, the Fire & Safety/Diversified Products (FSDP) segment faces challenges due to softness in the dispensing business and decreased demand for fire and rescue tools, alongside foreign currency exchange rate risks.

feed.title: Ford recalls 600,000 vehicles. See affected models.
feed.summary: Ford has issued a recall for over 600,000 vehicles, including certain 2020-2022 models of the Explorer, Escape, Lincoln Aviator, and Lincoln Corsair, due to a faulty windshield wiper motor that could increase the risk of a crash. Dealerships will replace the affected motors for free. The company is not aware of any accidents or injuries related to this particular defect.

feed.title: Fastenal stock sees BofA reiterate Buy on February sales strength
feed.summary: BofA Securities reiterated a Buy rating and a $48.00 price target for Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) after the company reported strong February sales performance, with daily sales growth up 13.3% year-over-year. This acceleration in sales, coupled with a 27% return over the past year and a 34-year dividend streak, highlights the company's strong momentum despite some analysts noting potential overvaluation and recent earnings missing some projections. Other analysts from Bernstein, UBS, and Morgan Stanley offer varying outlooks on the stock based on recent financial reports and sales figures.

feed.title: DE Gains 33% in a Year: What's the Right Strategy for Investors Now?
feed.summary: Deere & Company (DE) shares have risen 33.1% in the past year, outperforming its industry and the S&P 500, but faces challenges from low crop prices and tariffs impacting its near-term earnings. Despite these headwinds and a premium valuation, the company's long-term growth prospects remain solid due to consistent innovation, geographic expansion, and strategic acquisitions in agricultural and construction technology. Investors are advised to stay invested if they already own the stock but new investors should wait for a more favorable entry point.

feed.title: Caterpillar Inc Stock (CAT) Moved Down by 3.02% on Mar 5: Facts Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc.'s (CAT) stock declined by 3.02% due to concerns over elevated valuation, persistent tariff-driven cost pressures, and broader macroeconomic factors like sticky inflation. Despite strong recent financial performance, analysts suggest the stock's premium valuation and the impact of tariffs on operating margins are weighing on investor sentiment. Technical indicators show a neutral to oversold condition, while fundamental analysis highlights strong revenue and profit but also significant debt.

feed.title: Iran war depletes US weapons, munitions; where they're made near you
feed.summary: The ongoing conflict in Iran is causing a depletion of U.S. weapons and munitions, prompting a need to ramp up production from defense contractors. Major companies like Lockheed Martin, RTX, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics, and Boeing have production facilities in the Mid-Atlantic region that contribute to the U.S. defense supply. President Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with defense industry executives to address rapidly replenishing the nation's stockpile.

feed.title: Ametek (AME) Up 3% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
feed.summary: Ametek (AME) shares have increased by 3% since its last earnings report, surpassing the S&P 500. The company reported strong Q4 2025 results, with both earnings and revenues exceeding estimates, driven by growth in its EIG and EMG segments. Ametek has also provided positive guidance for 2026 and Q1 2026, anticipating mid to high single-digit sales growth and increased adjusted earnings per share.

feed.title: Stratasys acquires Arevo 3D printing assets and IP
feed.summary: Stratasys has acquired the technology portfolio and intellectual property (IP) of carbon fiber 3D printing firm Arevo, following an asset auction. Arevo, known for its AQUA 3D printers and later its SuperStrata bikes, ceased operations last year despite significant funding. Stratasys plans to leverage this IP, which includes carbon fiber 3D printing and AI-powered monitoring, to enhance its FDM technology, improve part isotropy, and boost 3D printer throughput for manufacturing applications.

feed.title: Methode Electronics Announces Sale of dataMate Business
feed.summary: Methode Electronics, Inc. has announced the sale of its dataMate copper transceiver business to Bel Fuse Inc. This divestiture aligns with Methode's strategy to refine its portfolio, reduce debt, and focus on high-density power delivery products for data centers and AI computing architectures. The sale is expected to allow dataMate to further expand its innovative data-over-copper solutions as part of the Bel Fuse portfolio.

feed.title: TSMC, Samsung Lead Recovery In Asia's Emerging Markets With AI And Chip Sector Growth
feed.summary: Emerging markets, particularly in Asia, are recovering from 2022 losses, driven by tech giants like TSMC and Samsung, despite global headwinds. The recovery is fueled by demand in the AI and semiconductor sectors, offering attractive valuations compared to developed markets. While China risk remains, alternative ETFs excluding China provide options for investors.

feed.title: Honeywell takes next step in aerospace spinoff, offers more details
feed.summary: Honeywell has filed a Form 10 registration statement, providing more details about the upcoming spinoff of its aerospace division. This move is a significant step in the regulatory process, with the offshoot expected to become a publicly traded company on Nasdaq under the ticker "HONA" in the third quarter of 2026. Honeywell aims to focus on its core industrial and automation segments, while the new aerospace entity, which generated $17.4 billion in sales in 2025, will concentrate on efficiency, safety, and connectivity in commercial and defense aviation.

feed.title: Nvidia investing $2B in Coherent
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. is investing $2 billion in Saxonburg-based Coherent Corp., a manufacturer of semiconductors and lasers, as part of a larger $4 billion investment in photonic technology companies. This investment also includes a nonexclusive agreement where Nvidia will purchase advanced laser and optical products from Coherent to support R&D and expand Coherent’s U.S. manufacturing. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that this partnership aims to pioneer next-generation silicon photonics for AI infrastructure, emphasizing speed and energy efficiency.

feed.title: New $35,000 pontoon aims to get first-time boaters on the water
feed.summary: Winnebago's Barletta Boats has launched the Sanza, a new pontoon model designed for first-time and value-minded buyers, with a nationally advertised starting price under $35,000. It features a streamlined design, two floorplans, and a maximum 150-horsepower rating, while maintaining Barletta's structural quality and customer support, including a Summer Hotline. The Sanza aims to simplify boat ownership and expand access to the on-water lifestyle through authorized dealers nationwide.

feed.title: Barletta Boats introduces the Sanza: Pontoons with smart design and trusted quality in a streamlined package
feed.summary: Barletta Boats has launched its new Sanza pontoon series, designed for first-time and value-minded buyers seeking trusted quality without complexity. The Sanza offers streamlined design, essential features, and Barletta's structural integrity, available with two floorplans and a starting price under $35,000. It emphasizes ease of ownership and customer support, including access to Barletta's Summer Hotline.

feed.title: IDEX Stock Exhibits Strong Prospects Despite Persisting Headwinds
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation (IEX) is experiencing strong performance in its Fluid & Metering Technologies (FMT) and Health & Science Technologies (HST) segments, driven by increased demand and strategic acquisitions. Despite headwinds in its Fire & Safety/Diversified Products (FSDP) segment and foreign currency risks, the company continues to reward shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. IDEX holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) and its shares have outperformed the industry in the past three months.

feed.title: Dow jumps as analysts turn more constructive amid oil-driven sentiment shift
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (DOW) experienced a 6.6% increase in its stock price, driven by renewed analyst optimism. This shift includes ratings upgrades and higher price targets from firms like BMO Capital Markets and KeyBanc, partly influenced by rising oil prices and geopolitical factors favoring U.S.-based chemical producers. The article also highlights significant institutional investor activity and congressional trading in DOW stock.

feed.title: Ex-Dividend Reminder: Allison Transmission Holdings, Global Industrial and Strategic Education
feed.summary: Allison Transmission Holdings (ALSN), Global Industrial Company (GIC), and Strategic Education Inc (STRA) are set to trade ex-dividend on March 9, 2026. This means investors buying shares on or after that date will not receive the upcoming dividends. The companies will pay quarterly dividends of $0.29, $0.28, and $0.60 respectively, with the stock prices expected to adjust lower by the dividend amount on the ex-dividend date.

feed.title: KBR Secures 10-Year Catalyst Supply Agreement with Indorama for Global Ammonia Assets
feed.summary: KBR has secured a 10-year contract with Indorama Eleme Fertilizer & Chemicals FZE to supply catalysts for Indorama's six global ammonia plants across Nigeria, Georgia, Uzbekistan, and India. This agreement marks KBR's first long-term catalyst supply contract in the ammonia sector, strengthening its role as a provider of integrated ammonia technologies. The partnership aims to enhance Indorama's plant performance, operational efficiency, and consistent ammonia output through KBR's advanced catalyst systems and technical support.

feed.title: Taylor Mike J, Friedman Industries CEO, buys $7200 in FRD stock
feed.summary: Friedman Industries Inc. CEO, Taylor Mike J., purchased 400 shares of FRD common stock on March 3, 2026, for $7,200, increasing his direct ownership to 222,666 shares. This insider buying follows the company's recent declaration of a quarterly cash dividend of $0.04 per share and an increase in its borrowing capacity from $125 million to $140 million through an amended credit facility.

feed.title: Badger Meter VP Sells $309,600 in Stock
feed.summary: Badger Meter, Inc. VP Fred Begale sold 2,064 shares of company stock for $309,600, reducing his ownership by over 40%. This transaction occurred after the company missed quarterly earnings and revenue expectations, and it raises questions about management's outlook, though it could also be for personal financial planning. Investors are advised to monitor Badger Meter's financial performance and analyst commentary closely.

feed.title: Do Options Traders Have Insights on Arcosa Stock That We're Unaware Of?
feed.summary: Arcosa, Inc. (ACA) has shown unusual options activity, particularly with its April 17, 2026, $70.00 call option displaying high implied volatility, suggesting expectations of significant price movement. Despite this, analyst sentiment for Arcosa is bearish, with a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) and recent downward revisions to earnings forecasts. This divergence might present a trading opportunity for experienced options traders who sell premium.

feed.title: RSI Alert: Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) Now Oversold
feed.summary: Axalta Coating Systems (AXTA) has entered oversold territory with an RSI reading of 27.3, falling below the typical threshold of 30. This technical indicator suggests that the stock has experienced heavy selling, which some bullish investors might view as a potential entry point. AXTA's shares traded as low as $30.12, compared to its 52-week range of $26.28 to $35.96.

feed.title: Greif Class A Refinances and Restructures Secured Credit Facilities
feed.summary: Greif, Inc. (GEF) has refinanced and restructured its secured credit facilities by replacing its prior senior secured credit agreement with a new third amended and restated agreement on February 27, 2026. This move involved fully repaying and terminating its earlier syndicated and CoBank credit agreements, aiming to enhance financial flexibility and align its capital structure with current operational needs. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates GEF as Neutral due to weak cash generation despite improved leverage and solid profitability.

feed.title: Fastenal’s February: Continued Sales Acceleration Tops Expectations
feed.summary: Fastenal reported strong February sales, with total sales reaching $711 million, up 13.3% year-over-year on a daily sales basis, surpassing analyst estimates. This marks the company's best daily sales growth since October 2022 and its eighth consecutive month of double-digit growth. The acceleration occurred despite a decrease in the percentage of top national accounts showing growth, driven by strong performance in various geographies, product categories, and end markets.

feed.title: FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of FuelCell Energy, Advanced Drainage, Northwest Pipe, Bel Fuse, and JELD-WEN significantly dropped due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring and reignited inflation concerns. The broader market experienced a substantial sell-off, impacting various sectors, with Brent crude nearing $85 a barrel. JELD-WEN, in particular, saw a 7.7% fall, despite recent positive operational performance in its Q4 earnings, highlighting the market's sensitivity to global events.

feed.title: General Motors prepares to launch ‘SDVerse’ automotive software marketplace
feed.summary: General Motors, in partnership with Magna and Wipro, is set to launch SDVerse, a B2B automotive software marketplace. This platform aims to create an "automotive app store" for software developers to sell products to car manufacturers, addressing current inefficiencies in vehicle software development and implementation. It is expected to make automotive software development cheaper and faster.

feed.title: Why Sanmina (SANM) Shares Are Falling Today

feed.title: RBC Capital Upgrades Builders FirstSource (BLDR) to Outperform

feed.title: Quanex: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Quanex Building Products Corp. (NX) reported a loss of $4.1 million in its fiscal first quarter, amounting to 9 cents per share. feed.title: Fincantieri Delivers Norwegian Luna
feed.summary: Fincantieri has delivered the Norwegian Luna to Norwegian Cruise Line at its Marghera shipyard in Venice, Italy. This vessel is the second in the expanded Prima Plus Class, following the Norwegian Aqua delivered in 2025. The Norwegian Luna is 10% larger than the original Prima-class ships, measuring 156,000 gross tons and offering over 3,550 berths.

feed.title: Analysts Push Dow Inc. Higher Amid Supply Shock
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (DOW) shares rose due to expectations of increased profits from its plastics business, driven by a global polyethylene supply disruption caused by the Iran conflict. Analysts have upgraded the stock and raised price targets, citing reduced short-term risk and improved earnings prospects. This situation is attracting traders seeking defensive growth in the chemicals sector.

feed.title: Fincantieri Delivers Norwegian Luna Cruise Vessel
feed.summary: Fincantieri has delivered the Norwegian Luna, the second vessel in Norwegian Cruise Line's expanded Prima Plus class, at its Marghera shipyard in Italy. This 156,000-gt ship is approximately 10% larger than previous Prima-class vessels and features new entertainment and recreation concepts, including the Aqua Slidecoaster and Ocean Boulevard. The delivery follows a new agreement between Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. and Fincantieri to build three next-generation cruise ships, with deliveries scheduled through 2037.

feed.title: Fastenal (FAST) Sees 13.3% Increase in February Sales
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) reported a significant 13.3% increase in net sales for February, reaching over $710 million, with daily sales also climbing by the same percentage. This growth is attributed to the company's strategy of expanding on-site locations and its role as a comprehensive outsourcing partner for industrial customers. Fastenal demonstrates strong financial health with impressive revenue growth, profitability metrics, and a conservative approach to leverage, though its valuation metrics indicate a premium trading position.

feed.title: This Senator Just Sold Up To $60K In Illinois Tool Works Stock
feed.summary: Senator Shelley M Capito recently sold Illinois Tool Works stock valued between $4,004 and $60,000 on February 24, 2026. This transaction is part of her active trading history, which includes 144 trades totaling over $284,000 in the past three years. Congressional transactions, like this one, are publicly disclosed to ensure transparency and prevent insider trading as mandated by the STOCK Act.

feed.title: BorgWarner Battery Management System Selected for Production
feed.summary: BorgWarner's battery management system (BMS) has been chosen for an expanded series-production program by a global OEM, extending its use to additional B-segment and C-segment passenger cars, and light commercial vehicles for BEV and PHEV applications starting in 2029. The modular BMS monitors charge level, battery health, and temperatures, performing passive cell balancing to optimize performance. Its scalable architecture supports diverse BEV and PHEV battery pack designs and operates up to 800 volts, adhering to ASIL D functional safety goals.

feed.title: Mercury Marine Fighting E15
feed.summary: Mercury Marine, in conjunction with its parent company Brunswick Corp. and the National Marine Manufacturers Association (NMMA), is actively supporting the “Look Before You Pump” campaign. This initiative aims to dissuade powerboat owners from using E15 gasoline in their marine engines due to its proven damaging effects. The campaign seeks to inform boaters about the risks, as E15 can void engine warranties and lead to engine failure, often in dangerous on-water situations.

feed.title: AeroVironment, Inc. to Present at J.P. Morgan 2026 Industrials Conference
feed.summary: AeroVironment, Inc. (NASDAQ: AVAV) announced it will participate in the J.P. Morgan 2026 Industrials Conference. The company's chairman, president, and CEO, Wahid Nawabi, and executive vice president and CFO, Kevin McDonnell, will host a fireside chat on March 18 at 1:35 p.m. ET. A webcast of the event will be available on the company's website.

feed.title: AeroVironment $30M Expansion for Defense Plant
feed.summary: AeroVironment, Inc. announced a $30 million expansion of its Albuquerque, New Mexico manufacturing site, dedicated to producing directed energy laser systems for the Pentagon. This significant investment is expected to create 450 jobs and is supported by $6 million in state and local incentives. The expansion aims to strengthen the domestic defense industrial base, create high-quality jobs, and foster an innovation and manufacturing hub in New Mexico.

feed.title: The Toro Company (NYSE:TTC) Surpasses Q4 CY2025 Projections
feed.summary: The Toro Company (NYSE:TTC) exceeded market expectations for Q4 CY2025 with $1.04 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of $0.74 per share. feed.title: The Toro Company (NYSE:TTC) Exceeds Q4 CY2025 Expectations
feed.summary: The Toro Company (NYSE:TTC) reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, with revenue of $1.04 billion surpassing analyst expectations and non-GAAP profit per share of $0.74 significantly beating estimates. feed.title: H.B. Fuller talks advanced barrier coatings portfolio, Coativ™ rebrand and more
feed.summary: H.B. Fuller has rebranded its barrier coatings portfolio as Coativ™ to better distinguish it from its adhesives business and highlight its commitment to sustainable packaging innovation. The company's barrier coatings are designed to help brand owners meet recyclability standards with fiber-based packaging, offering improved fiber yield during repulping compared to polyethylene coatings. Through extensive testing and customer collaboration, Coativ™ solutions are enabling companies to replace plastic barriers in various applications while maintaining performance and enhancing production efficiency.

feed.title: EMCOR Group, Inc. Announces Participation in Upcoming Investor Event
feed.summary: EMCOR Group, Inc. announced that its Chairman, President, and CEO, Tony Guzzi, along with Senior Vice President and CFO, Jason Nalbandian, and Director of Financial Planning & Analysis, Lucas Sullivan, will participate in the Cantor Global Technology & Industrial Growth Conference on March 11, 2026, in New York, New York. The presentation will be available via a live audio webcast and archived on the company's website until April 11, 2026. feed.title: IPG Photonics, PPG and Whirlpool partner to commercialize laser powder curing
feed.summary: IPG Photonics, PPG, and Whirlpool Corporation have partnered to commercialize laser curing systems for powder coatings. This collaboration aims to achieve faster, more energy-efficient curing processes compared to conventional methods, with PPG opening a pilot finishing line featuring IPG’s PhotoniCURE system. The partnership seeks to reduce curing costs and environmental impact while increasing finishing line speed, highlighting a significant advancement in the coatings industry.

feed.title: Paz Maestas Of DXP Enterprises Shows Optimism, Buys $220K In Stock

feed.title: Ultralife Corporation launches X5 SuperLite in Las Vegas

Developed in partnership with Karta Healthcare, the X5-SuperLite uses hot-swap batteries and can power up to two USB-C compatible devices simultaneously, with options for additional units and a state-of-charge indicator. The system is designed to provide uninterruptible power with hold-up batteries for backup.

feed.title: General Dynamics Mission Systems awarded contract to support Trident II fire control system through 2032
feed.summary: General Dynamics Mission Systems has been awarded a follow-on contract worth an initial $255 million, potentially rising to $740 million, to continue as the prime integrator for the Trident II Fire Control System. The contract, awarded in January 2026, ensures the company provides full life cycle and operational support for Ohio-class ballistic missile submarine fire control systems and develops, produces, and installs systems for new Columbia-class submarines through 2032. This work is critical for maintaining the U.S. Navy's strategic deterrent capabilities and modernizing its shipbuilding priorities.

feed.title: Dow, Inc. Hits Day High with Strong 5.2% Intraday Surge

Despite this recent uplift, the company faces considerable long-term financial challenges, including negative operating cash flow, declining pre-tax profits, and a low return on capital employed. However, its high dividend yield of 222.37% may attract investors despite these underlying issues.

feed.title: Ford Recalls 605K SUVs Due to Windshield Wiper Issue
feed.summary: Ford is recalling nearly 605,000 SUVs, including various Escape, Explorer, Corsair, and Aviator models from 2020-2022, due to a windshield wiper motor defect that can cause them to work intermittently or fail. The issue stems from a poor electrical connection caused by a misalignment in the motor's cover terminal. Owners will be notified by mail to have the motors inspected and replaced free of charge at a Ford or Lincoln dealer. This is Ford's second major recall in ten days.

feed.title: DuPont Upgrades WAVE PRO Capabilities With RO and NI Tech
feed.summary: DuPont has enhanced its WAVE PRO water treatment modeling tool by integrating reverse osmosis (RO) and nanofiltration (NF) capabilities. This upgrade allows the platform to combine UF, IX, RO, and NF technologies for diverse water treatment applications, helping users optimize system configurations, improve operating costs, and reduce environmental impact. The advanced tool, which is widely used by water professionals, brings together various DuPont Water Solutions technologies to purify over 50 million gallons of water every minute globally.

Investors are advised to consider this sentiment alongside APD's fundamentals, project pipeline, and analyst outlook.

This collaboration focuses on enabling advanced 3D perception and safety awareness in challenging environments, allowing robots to transition from simulation to production-ready, safety-capable operation. TI will demonstrate their joint solution at NVIDIA GTC, scheduled for March 16–19, 2026.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Unveils the STM32C5, an Entry-Level 40nm Microcontroller Range at a Keen Price

feed.title: Utah brine by-product tapped for first US lithium project of its kind

The design and execution are being carried out under a joint development agreement with Aquatech and Adionics, utilizing Aquatech's modular PEARL™ process for capital efficiency and scalability.

feed.title: Lawmakers Question Security of Intel's ACM Research Chip Tool Testing - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Bipartisan U.S. lawmakers have raised national security concerns regarding Intel's testing of chipmaking tools from ACM Research. The lawmakers worry that ACM Research, which has ties to sanctioned Chinese entities, could expose Intel's cutting-edge chipmaking processes to Chinese military companies. Intel maintains that ACM tools are not used in its production and that it fully complies with U.S. regulations.

feed.title: Wolfspeed chip aims for 30% cheaper power to feed AI data centers

This new technology promises to reduce system costs by approximately 30%, improve power density by over 300%, and decrease thermal requirements by up to 50%, while offering unparalleled reliability with 158,000 years of predicted operation. The innovation aims to accelerate the transition to more efficient power conversion and support the growing demands of AI data centers.

feed.title: This Egyptian Army ATV is Actually a Polaris Snowmobile With Wheels and a Machine Gun
feed.summary: The Egyptian army is reportedly using an "ATV" that is actually a modified Polaris snowmobile equipped with wheels and a machine gun, drawing attention for its unconventional design. This vehicle, identified as a "Sand-X DOV" built by German company Eiselin Landsystems, is designed to be a "Dual-Operating Vehicle" capable of transitioning between snow and dirt terrain. The article questions the practicality of a snowmobile conversion in a desert environment and notes a peculiar driver-gunner setup where the driver might be exposed to hot brass from the front-mounted machine gun.

feed.title: The Eastern Company (NASDAQ:EML) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: The Eastern Company (NASDAQ:EML) discussed its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, highlighting a challenging year due to end market pressures in heavy truck and automotive sectors. Despite a 9% revenue decline for the year, the company made significant operational progress, including cost base reductions, portfolio streamlining, and strengthening its balance sheet. Management expressed cautious optimism for 2026, noting strengthening commercial pipelines and improving market conditions after 2025 was spent building a stronger foundation.

feed.title: $27M boost for sole US antimony producer after China export ban
feed.summary: United States Antimony (UAMY) has received a $27 million award from the Department of War under the Defense Production Act to expand and modernize its antimony extraction, processing, and refinement capabilities. This investment, which includes a $3.9 million company cost share, aims to boost output to 400-500 tons/month by April 2026 and complements an existing $248 million sole-source contract with the DLA Strategic Materials. The move is critical for U.S. supply chain resilience given China's 2024 export ban on antimony, strengthening domestic critical mineral production for defense and industrial needs.

feed.title: 1,424 Shares in Parker-Hannifin Corporation $PH Acquired by Integrated Quantitative Investments LLC
feed.summary: Integrated Quantitative Investments LLC has acquired 1,424 shares of Parker-Hannifin Corporation, valued at approximately $1.08 million, marking a new position for the firm. This acquisition contributes to institutional investors owning 82.44% of the company's stock, despite recent insider selling activities totaling over $12 million. Parker-Hannifin has shown strong financial performance, beating quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, and analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $995.37.

feed.title: Greene Concepts Inc. Reaffirms Commitment to Source Protection and Quality Standards for Be Water
feed.summary: Greene Concepts Inc. (INKW) emphasizes its rigorous standards for sourcing and quality in its Be Water brand. The premium artesian spring water is drawn from a protected aquifer in the Blue Ridge Mountains, ensuring purity through natural filtration and controlled, on-site bottling. CEO Lenny Greene highlights the company's commitment to source integrity and consistent quality to build consumer trust.

feed.title: US lawmakers raise concerns about Intel's testing of tools from China-linked firm
feed.summary: A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers, including Senator Elizabeth Warren and Representative Tom Cotton, has voiced national security concerns regarding Intel's testing of chipmaking tools from ACM Research, a firm with significant ties to China. The lawmakers fear that ACM Research's access to Intel's facilities could expose cutting-edge chipmaking processes to Chinese military companies, especially since some of ACM's affiliates and customers, like YMTC and SMIC, are under U.S. sanctions. Intel stated that ACM tools are not used in its semiconductor production processes and that it fully complies with U.S. laws, while ACM Research maintains it poses no national security threat and protects customer trade secrets.

feed.title: Integrated Quantitative Investments LLC Purchases Shares of 3,197 General Dynamics Corporation $GD
feed.summary: Integrated Quantitative Investments LLC has acquired 3,197 shares of General Dynamics Corporation, valued at approximately $1.09 million, during the third quarter. General Dynamics recently reported strong Q4 results, exceeding analyst estimates with $4.17 EPS and $14.38 billion in revenue, driven by its Marine Systems segment. The company also provided a positive FY2026 guidance, and institutional investors collectively own 86.14% of the stock.

feed.title: Geo Capital Gestora de Recursos Ltd Sells 6,749 Shares of Deere & Company $DE
feed.summary: Geo Capital Gestora de Recursos Ltd significantly reduced its stake in Deere & Company (NYSE:DE) by 62.6% in the third quarter, selling 6,749 shares and retaining 4,029 shares valued at $1.842 million. Despite this sale, Deere & Company remains the firm's 17th-largest holding, accounting for 3.4% of its portfolio. The article also notes that Deere recently beat earnings expectations and analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock.

feed.title: Astronics Rallies 46% YTD: Should Investors Bet on the Stock Now?
feed.summary: Astronics Corporation (ATRO) shares have rallied 46.2% year-to-date, outperforming its industry and sector. The company, a provider of aerospace and defense technologies, is considered undervalued and is expected to benefit from favorable demand conditions and its strong product portfolio. Analysts show optimistic sentiment for its future revenue and earnings growth.

feed.title: KCM Investment Advisors LLC Sells 6,650 Shares of Deere & Company $DE
feed.summary: KCM Investment Advisors LLC decreased its stake in Deere & Company (NYSE:DE) by 46.0% in the third quarter, selling 6,650 shares and retaining 7,822 shares valued at $3,577,000. Despite this reduction, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc. and State Street Corp increased their holdings. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Deere & Company, with a target price of $647.20.

feed.title: What’s next for First Solar?
feed.summary: First Solar's share price recently dropped following its 2025 results and 2026 guidance, despite significant investments in U.S. manufacturing capacity and a shift towards domestic production. The company is restructuring its Southeast Asian operations into front-end production hubs, with finished modules assembled in South Carolina to leverage 45X tax credits. Wall Street's reaction was unexpected, given the company's clear disclosures and strengthened financial position post-IRA, suggesting a misinterpretation of its strategic recalibration.

feed.title: Samsung Display Wins Largest Share of OLED Orders for Apple's iPhone 17e
feed.summary: Samsung Display has secured the majority of OLED panel orders for Apple's new entry-level iPhone 17e, overturning earlier predictions that BOE would be the main supplier. This allocation is largely due to reported production quality issues at BOE, which have led to temporary halts in their iPhone OLED production. LG Display and BOE are expected to supply the remaining panels, with Samsung Display maintaining its dominant position similar to the iPhone 16e orders.

feed.title: Amprius Technologies, Inc. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary
feed.summary: Amprius Technologies, Inc. reported a significant turnaround in Q4 2025, achieving 137% year-over-year revenue growth driven by the adoption of its SiCore silicon anode batteries. The company improved gross margins, strategically exited its Colorado facility, secured a major DIU contract, and expanded its customer base. For 2026, Amprius projects at least $125 million in revenue and anticipates its first full year of positive adjusted EBITDA, while focusing on long-term scalability and NDAA compliance.

feed.title: Dow Inc. Stock: Quiet Materials Giant Making a Loud Comeback?
feed.summary: Dow Inc. (DOW) is being re-evaluated by investors, moving beyond its traditional image as a chemical stock. The company is focusing on high-margin materials, AI-optimized manufacturing, and a strong dividend strategy, making it an attractive option for income-focused and cyclical investors in the US. While not a high-growth "AI stock," Dow offers potential for stable returns as industrial activity recovers and demand for specialized materials in sectors like EVs and sustainable packaging increases.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Boeing: "It's The Right Stock, Right Time"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer views Boeing (BA) as "the right stock, right time" despite recent declines, attributing the drop to concerns about travel and the end of the bull market, as well as Middle East tensions. He maintains a significant position in his Trust, suggesting the stock should be bought due to its record orders. Cramer also acknowledged challenges like inefficiencies in defense contracts but highlighted Boeing's growing backlog.

feed.title: HawkSearch By Bridgeline Enhances eCommerce Search for Global Industrial and Medical Gas Provider
feed.summary: Bridgeline Digital, Inc. announced that a global industrial and medical gas provider has selected its HawkSearch platform to enhance eCommerce search capabilities. HawkSearch will provide advanced on-site search, product discovery, multi-language support, and personalized customer journeys to improve engagement and conversion rates. The solution is designed to handle complex product catalogs and business requirements for global organizations.

feed.title: GW&K Investment Management LLC Trims Stock Holdings in Nordson Corporation $NDSN
feed.summary: GW&K Investment Management LLC has reduced its stake in Nordson Corporation (NASDAQ:NDSN) by 3.9% in the third quarter, now owning 361,428 shares valued at $82,027,000. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions, and hedge funds collectively own 72.11% of the company's stock. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $311.50, while company insiders have recently sold over $21 million worth of shares.

feed.title: Ronal Group standardizes remote access with Rockwell tools
feed.summary: Ronal Group, a major manufacturer of vehicle wheels, has adopted Rockwell Automation's secure remote access technology to standardize and centralize remote access across its global operations. This move aims to enhance operational excellence and comply with regulatory requirements, including NIS2, by implementing encrypted connections, role-based permissions, and comprehensive monitoring. The solution ensures consistent management of authorized external access, with rights assigned based on clear roles and operational needs.

feed.title: GW&K Investment Management LLC Raises Position in Applied Industrial Technologies, Inc. $AIT
feed.summary: GW&K Investment Management LLC has increased its stake in Applied Industrial Technologies by 2.9% in the third quarter of 2025, now holding 211,182 shares valued at approximately $55.13 million. The company reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend. Analyst sentiment toward AIT is largely positive, with several firms issuing "buy" or "overweight" ratings and an average price target of $294.38.

feed.title: Ambiq Reveals 12nm SPOT® Platform Enabling Operation Down to 300mV for Atomiq™
feed.summary: Ambiq has unveiled technical details of its upcoming Atomiq™ SoC, built on a new 12nm SPOT® platform using the TSMC N12e® process. This platform introduces an Ultra-Low-Power (ULP) mode capable of operating as low as 300mV, the lowest in Ambiq's history, targeting enhanced energy efficiency for edge AI applications. Production of Atomiq110 samples is scheduled for 2027, with the innovation resulting from refined SPOT technology and architectural advancements.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Calls Carrier Global Corporation's Full-Year Forecast "Justifiably a Little Cautious"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Carrier Global Corporation's (NYSE: CARR) performance, noting that although its stock is up 16% in 2026, recent top and bottom-line results were "a little weaker than expected" due to North American residential construction weakness. He described the company as a "two-speed story" with a cyclical residential business and a strong commercial HVAC and aftermarket business, partly driven by data center exposure. Cramer stated that Carrier's full-year forecast was "justifiably a little cautious."

feed.title: Whirlpool Picks Schneider Logistics for Port Services
feed.summary: Whirlpool Corp. has selected Schneider Logistics' subsidiary, American Port Services (APS), to manage port transloading, warehousing, and distribution services near the Port of Savannah, Georgia. This partnership aims to enhance Whirlpool's product distribution from Europe to U.S. customers, involving over 380,000 appliances annually. Schneider Logistics will reconfigure 500,000 square feet in two warehouses to facilitate the transloading of German-manufactured front-loading washers, streamlining Whirlpool's supply chain by removing a distribution step.

feed.title: Whirlpool Corp. Works To Better Meet the Needs of Multicultural Consumers
feed.summary: Whirlpool Corp. is actively working to better understand and serve multicultural consumers through its "Cultural Intelligence Series" initiative. This research-driven approach involves deep dives into the daily lives and experiences of diverse groups, including Black, Latin-X, Asian American, LGBTQ+, and consumers with disabilities, to ensure authentic connections and reduce biases in marketing and product development. The company aims to embed its core values of inclusion and diversity into all aspects of the consumer journey, from ideation to service, to truly improve life at home for a broad array of diverse consumers.

feed.title: BMO upgrades Westlake Chemical stock rating on Iran supply impact
feed.summary: BMO Capital upgraded Westlake Chemical (NYSE:WLK) to Outperform from Market Perform and raised its price target to $127, citing the impact of the Iran conflict on global polyethylene supply, which is expected to tighten supply and demand dynamics. The firm anticipates an earnings recovery for Westlake due to supply constraints, a widening ethane advantage, and potential recovery in U.S. housing markets. This upgrade comes despite a recent significant earnings miss, as other analysts like BofA Securities also see potential for growth.

feed.title: Vishay Introduces VOx619A Series Phototransistor Optocouplers
feed.summary: Vishay Intertechnology Inc. has launched its new VOx619A series of phototransistor optocouplers, designed for industrial and electronic applications. These optocouplers offer high and linear current transfer ratio at a low forward current of 0.5 milliamps, reducing power consumption. They are designed for high-temperature operation up to +125 degrees Celsius and are available in various package types, providing enhanced signal isolation and reliability.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Calls Carrier Global Corporation’s Full-Year Forecast “Justifiably a Little Cautious”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Carrier Global Corporation's (NYSE: CARR) full-year forecast, calling it "justifiably a little cautious" due to weaker-than-expected top and bottom line results, influenced by a downturn in North American residential construction. Despite this, he noted the company's stock is up 16% in 2026 and highlighted strength in its commercial HVAC and aftermarket business, which benefits from data center exposure and aligns with the "heavy assets, low obsolescence" investment rubric.

feed.title: Segall Bryant & Hamill LLC Invests $8.11 Million in Timken Company (The) $TKR
feed.summary: Segall Bryant & Hamill LLC has acquired a new stake of 107,835 shares, valued at $8.11 million, in The Timken Company. This comes as Timken reported strong Q4 results, beating analyst estimates for EPS and revenue, and provided positive FY2026 guidance. Despite a director selling a portion of his shares, several other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in the company.

feed.title: HawkSearch By Bridgeline Enhances eCommerce Search for Global Industrial and Medical Gas Provider
feed.summary: Bridgeline Digital, Inc. announced that a major global industrial and medical gas provider has selected HawkSearch to enhance its eCommerce search capabilities. This implementation aims to improve product discoverability, enable complex business requirements such as multi-language search and custom pricing, and ultimately increase engagement and conversion rates across its digital channels. HawkSearch's AI-powered tools and robust merchandising features are expected to streamline operations and support scalable growth for the client.

feed.title: GW&K Investment Management LLC Has $132.10 Million Holdings in ITT Inc. $ITT
feed.summary: GW&K Investment Management LLC reduced its stake in ITT Inc. by 3.6% in the third quarter, selling 27,371 shares but still holding 738,993 shares valued at approximately $132.10 million, making ITT its sixth-largest holding. ITT recently beat quarterly earnings expectations with $1.85 EPS and $1.05 billion in revenue, announced increased dividends, and received a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts with an average target price of $216.11. The company's strong financial performance and positive analyst outlook suggest continued growth, despite the recent minor reduction in GW&K's stake.
